programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Jack discovers an old friend has become a blackmailing call girl, and her murder uncovers a trail of corruption among her rich and influential clients. Drama, starring Scott Baio

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan investigates the murder of his old friend Mayor Ridgeway. The motive appears political, but things are not as clear-cut as they seem. Crime drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Trouble shadows Kimble in the form of a devoted runaway who is sought by the police for commitment to an institution

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry Mason solves the murder of an embezzler and exposes a scheme to steal a valuable paint formula

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A white supremacist group take Alex and the employees of a TV station hostage, as revenge for Walker and Trivette arresting their leader

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella investigate the murder of the host of a high-stakes poker game, while Danny and Hawkes conduct inquiries into the mysterious death of a news channel's weather girl whose body has been found in Central Park - and discover an incriminating videotape at her office that could provide clues. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise and Melina Kanakaredes

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team, alongside the FBI and MI6, continues the international manhunt for a disgraced British spy who is seeking revenge on the agents he blames for his wife's death. A re-examination of the fugitive's conviction for selling military intelligence reveals that he may have been framed - and the identity of the only person who can clear his name

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part one of two. The agents investigate when two men are killed at a gun shop, but soon discover one of their colleagues could be at risk. However, when he comes face to face with the fugitive, he is tricked into answering a phone wired to a car bomb

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble saves the life of Maggie Tippet, the one-armed man's girlfriend

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: A young woman flees the church on her wedding day, but when her blackmailer is killed, the wrong person is accused of murder

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs sends DiNozzo and Ziva to retrieve top-secret information from a secure military base - a mission aimed at catching a mole in the NCIS ranks. Unfortunately, it soon becomes clear that the traitor is still at large - so the building is locked down and all agents are rounded up in a final effort to catch the culprit. Guest starring Jude Ciccolella

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: An eight-year-old crime proves a challenge for Walker and Trivette when a decomposed body of a young Indian boy is unearthed on a building site. Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony tries to piece together the events that led to him lying wounded in a hospital bed, having been shot in the chest through his bulletproof vest. Under the guidance of therapist Dr Rachel Cranston, his memory begins to return and suspicion falls on his ex-lover EJ Barrett. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Sarah Jane Morris

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Chameleon's planted clues lead the team on a wild goose chase as it searches for NSA agent Mike Atley, who the criminal claims to have in his custody. Callen is faced with a difficult decision when he is told to release the Chameleon in order to save Atley's life, and contemplates subduing his nemesis once and for all

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Having identified the mole in their ranks, the agents arrest the traitor's contact Ted Bankston, a former NSA agent working for a kidnapper code-named the Weatherman - who is making millions on the stock markets out of stolen military data. Drama, with Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Mark suspects an eminent senator of killing his own daughter, whose behaviour was jeopardising his re-election campaign. Determined to discover the truth, he calls in Amanda to ask her advice. Dick Van Dyke and Victoria Rowell star

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan and the District Attorney visit a school to investigate a drive-by shooting that is not all it seems. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Victoria Rowell

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Team Scorpion's new neighbour joins them on a mission to destroy a floating pile of trash in the sea, but they end up stranded on the quickly dissolving island

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A seemingly simple case goes sideways when Team Scorpion helps a father and daughter whose plane crashes nearby. Paige helps Walter navigate his feelings

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A cook from a US Navy destroyer tells McGee and Gibbs he believes someone is stalking him with the intention of trying to make him poison the crew, and a picture on a stolen phone leads the team to a group testing the psychological effects of surveillance. Meanwhile, Richard Parsons from the Inspector General's office closes in on Ziva as he investigates the death of Ilan Bodnar, the man who killed her father. Guest starring Colin Hanks (Gus Grimly in the Fargo TV series)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when Navy Seal Chad McBride's wife receives a package from Riyadh in Saudi Arabia containing her husband's severed head, which Palmer discovers contains very high levels of radiation. McBride's commanding officer reveals the soldier was on a top-secret reconnaissance mission and asks Vance not to disclose the details to anyone. Meanwhile, Richard Parsons tries to get Gibbs to take responsibility for the many unnecessary deaths for which he holds the NCIS team accountable

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The American crime drama returns with the team still reeling from the Department of Defence investigation, with Ziva, McGee and Tony having handed in their badges. Parsons continues his inquiries into Gibbs and his agents, but events take a turn when an explosion kills the Secretary of the US Navy and seriously injures the director of Homeland Security. When attempts are made on the lives of Gibbs and Tony, it appears that all of the NCIS agents are in mortal danger. Starring Mark Harmon and Colin Hanks

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Sarah Porter, the new Secretary of the Navy, charges Gibbs and the team with finding terrorist group the Brotherhood of Doubt, which was responsible for killing her predecessor. She expects the group's next target to be billionaire Tomas Mendez, who spends his money trying to defeat terrorism, but whose motives may not be entirely altruistic. Meanwhile, Tony flies to Tel Aviv, where new Mossad director Orli Elbaz reveals they have a report of a possible sighting of Ziva at a kibbutz she went to as a child. Crime drama, guest starring Marina Sirtis

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An explosion at an apartment building brings navy officer Terence Keith to the team's attention and it transpires he had been on medication for anxiety, a condition that had led to him being ejected from a pilot's training course. Ducky's profile of Keith suggests that he may be unstable and about to exact revenge for having his dream of becoming a fighter ace taken away from him, and evidence points to him intending to carry out a suicide attack

programme.title: Scorpion

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy lieutenant is found dead in the Pentagon's custodial closet, and surveillance footage indicates she was killed by a co-worker. However, Ziva and the team discover her supposed assailant actually died of a heart attack days before the crime was committed. Meanwhile, suspicions are raised regarding the eyeball left in Tony's drink, and the investigators' attention is fixed on the port-to-port murderer, who appears to still be at large

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony's one-time Baltimore PD partner Danny Price is murdered in a manner that suggests the port-to-port killer has struck again. However, a key difference in the way the victim was bound suggests the crime was actually the work of a copycat, forcing the investigators to consider whether Price had any enemies

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: New evidence reveals the serial killer has infiltrated the agency and is targeting a member of the NCIS team, prompting Gibbs to seek the guidance of his former mentor Mike Franks. A lead from Barrett's squad reveals the CIA has also been tracking the murderer - and its findings suggest the culprit could be an even deadlier foe than anticipated

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team attend the funeral of a fallen colleague before resuming their hunt for the man they believe to be the port-to-port killer. Vance frantically researches the suspect's background to learn more about his motivations and discover who his next target could be - but one of the investigators follows a clue that leads to danger

Drama, guest starring Jolene Blalock, with Andrea Parker and David James Elliott

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A photograph draws a colleague of Dr Sloan into a blackmail plot linked to the murder of a Hollywood madam. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy Public Affairs Officer is killed, and when the team takes up the case, both the man's wife and his mistress emerge as potential suspects. Each woman offering a very different take on the chain of events that led to the murder, and the investigators are forced to decide which of the two is telling the truth. Meanwhile, DiNozzo's father comes to the aid of a homeless woman, who makes a surprising claim about his relationship with her. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony finds himself in a difficult position when his identity is stolen. Worse yet, multiple fraudsters make use of his status as a special agent to blackmail US senators, and as the chain of events begins to spiral out of control, it is left to the real Dinozzo to bring the perpetrators to justice. Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs teams up with his old friend Senior FBI Agent TC Fornell after two British prisoners, including a former spy, escape captivity and flee to the US via a shipping container. Meanwhile, McGee is busy hunting for a new apartment, and is determined to discover how DiNozzo managed to snag his lavish home. Naval crime drama, guest starring Joe Spano

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A 14-year-old boy manages to foil a home invasion, but when Gibbs is called in to investigate, he suspects the teenager may be withholding crucial information. As the case makes progress, Gibbs turns his attention to helping the boy's mother, the wife of a naval officer, and his efforts result in an invitation to the White House to meet the First Lady (Michelle Obama, guest starring as herself). programme.title: CSI: Miami

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A man is garrotted beside a hotel pool surrounded by dozens of other sun-worshippers, yet none of them witnesses the attack because the killer struck during a solar eclipse, when all eyes were trained on the sky. programme.title: CSI: Miami

programme.desc: Drama from the pen of Sally Wainwright. Catherine Cawood is the sergeant on duty when accountant Kevin Weatherill enters her police station to report a crime - the kidnapping of his boss's daughter. Although he originally came up with the plan as a way of paying for his children's private education, he eventually backed out. However, his partner is dangerous local drug kingpin Ashley Cowgill - and he has gone ahead with the plot anyway. The case hits a nerve for Catherine, who has lost her own daughter and is determined to bring the captors to justice. Military adventure, starring Sean Bean and Pete Postlethwaite

programme.desc: Pru from the Embassy visits Barry in jail and reassures the gang they are doing everything they can to free him - but the Cubans have discovered his Russian connections and convinced themselves he is a spy. Neville feels the pressure as he is forced to home in on his target, and Oz faces a major setback in his relationship with Ofelia. Timothy Spall, Jimmy Nail, Tim Healy and Kevin Whately star

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: A terrified Neville is kidnapped, driven through the back streets of Havana and deposited at an empty house - where he is greeted by Pru from the Embassy, who asks him to trap rogue operative Tarquin. Meanwhile, there is still no sign of Barry being released from jail, and his deteriorating condition prompts Oz to make a heartbreaking decision. Kevin Whately, Timothy Spall and Jimmy Nail star

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Horticultural lecturer Rosemary Boxer is hired by a wealthy but ill businessman to help cure a disease causing his trees to lose their bark. She gets to know Laura Thyme, who turns up after a friend is killed in a suspicious car accident, and when the pair find the lord of the manor unconscious beneath his bedroom window, they must decide whether depression led him to attempt suicide or if there is a more sinister explanation. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal, Pam Ferris, David Mallinson and Roger Alborough

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: The amateur sleuths uncover the remains of a racehorse while engaged on horticultural work in the grounds of pop star Nev Connolly's supposedly haunted mansion. Before long, gruesome revelations are coming thick and fast as Nev is shot dead and more bones are discovered - this time belonging to a young woman. As they investigate further, Rosemary and Laura are plunged into a sinister mystery with roots deep in the past. Drama, guest starring Murray Head (Heartbeat) and Abigail Cruttenden (Benidorm)

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are asked to restore an ornate water garden cascade in the grounds of a health spa, where they are invited to join owner Frances Caldecott and her children for dinner. That evening, the duo discover Frances has been strangled and her jewellery stolen, and set out to catch the culprit. Drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Mike and the team are reacquainted with some familiar faces when investigating a suspicious suicide at the Brokenwood Women's Prison. The hunt for the killer is complicated by apparent links to a spate of hate crimes by racist extremists in the city centre. Gritty crime thriller, starring John Hannah and James Cosmo

programme.desc: Drama based on the best-selling books by Henning Mankell, starring Kenneth Branagh as a Swedish detective recently estranged from his wife and struggling to readjust while fighting a rising tide of violence. Inspector Kurt Wallander searches for a link between three apparently motiveless murders, and tries to identify a girl who doused herself in petrol and burned to death. With Sarah Smart and Sadie Shimmin

The detective stages a press appeal, hoping to learn more about the mysterious pair and reveal their roles in both cases. Crime drama, starring Nathaniel Parker and Sharon Small

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Trixie is chuffed when local curate Tom invites her to join him for a day of cricket, sending her to work with a spring in her step. Is love on the cards for the young midwife? Shelagh's infertility has made her think long and hard about what she wants in life, so she approaches her husband with an idea, while Patsy upsets a young mother in the clinic and is moved to the district rota, where she works tirelessly to diagnose a docker's unusual symptoms

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Cynthia is concerned during a visit as she feels new mum Pamela is overly anxious, so Dr Turner arranges for a psychiatrist to come in. However, they soon discover both mother and baby have gone missing. Meanwhile, Jenny, on secondment, begins to see cracks in the establishment, Chummy discovers her mother has walked out on her father, and Trixie struggles to hide her disappointment when Tom cancels a date. Guest starring Sophie Rundle (The Bletchley Circle)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Jenny is assigned to first-time mother Jeanette, who requires frequent visits during a protracted labour, and when she meets her patient's cousin, Philip, a new friendship begins to grow for the midwife. Meanwhile, Chummy worries about her mother's illness when she discovers Lady Browne has discharged herself from hospital. Jessica Raine and Miranda Hart star

programme.desc: Drama from the best-selling author. Set between the wars, it concerns a girl who returns to her Tyneside home newly married to her black husband, only to encounter bigotry from all sides, to say nothing of her obsessed brother's animosity. Niamh Cusack, Tony Armatrading and Dearbhla Molloy star

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Dr Turner is reunited with a couple whose daughter was born without limbs, and takes them to a specialist unit where she can be fitted for prosthetics. However, while the experience helps the girl's mother to realise that she is not alone, it proves traumatic for the father, who blames his wife for his child's condition. Nurse Crane takes charge of Sister Winifred's driving lessons, and Trixie thinks Christopher is hiding something from her

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. An unpopular colonel is found dead in the vicarage of St Mary Mead and investigations reveal no shortage of suspects - including the vicar and members of the victim's family. Christmas edition from 1986, starring Joan Hickson, Paul Eddington and James Hazeldine

programme.title: The Casual Vacancy
programme.desc: Drama adapted from the novel by JK Rowling, set in an English village whose seemingly idyllic facade hides a community at war. At the parish council meeting, chairman Howard Mollison introduces his plans to turn the community centre into a health spa, but he reckons without local solicitor Barry Fairbrother, who makes an impassioned speech about the importance of the centre to the impoverished people of the nearby estate. However, an unexpected turn of events leaves an empty seat on the council - leading to the biggest battle Pagford has ever seen. Michael Gambon, Rory Kinnear, Keeley Hawes and Julia McKenzie star

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: When Penny's (Hayley McElhinney) away on honeymoon Hugh (Rodger Corser) and Tara (Kate Jenkinson) come out to play, with a little help from Charlie (Nicole da Silva) their night takes a dramatic turn as Hugh loses more than his sobriety

programme.title: The Heart Guy
programme.desc: April is pregnant, and not everyone is as supportive as Matt had hoped, but the netball game between the hospital and mining company takes all the focus

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The detective struggles to cope following the death of his wife and suffers more grief when the body of his godson is discovered in a bricked-up drain 12 years after he went missing. Lynley himself becomes a suspect when the boy's sister is found dead, forcing him to enlist the help of his lawyer and DS McCaffrey's son to prove his innocence. Crime drama, starring Nathaniel Parker, Sharon Small and Nicholas Farrell

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Burke and the team investigate university lecturer and crime novelist Mark Joffe, whose debut publication includes the detailed description of a murder identical to that of a real-life unsolved case. As the case unfolds, Burke becomes convinced of the writer's guilt, but cannot seem to find sufficient evidence to charge him. Guest starring Finlay Robertson (Life Begins), with Alex Norton, Blythe Duff and John Michie

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford report on the online electronics retailer that has left dozens of customers in the lurch. Plus, Julia hears from the learners in lockdown who found self-education more expensive than they'd expected, explaining how to avoid falling foul of the small print

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development progress of terraced houses in Carlisle and Abbey Wood in London, as well as a seven-year project to renovate a large period house in Torquay

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley reports on the dedicated fraud-fighters pursuing the criminals who steal £40billion a year from the public purse. This edition features the amateur actress who put on a performance to evade justice for a showstopping £750,000 fraud and the school business manager who embezzled over £200,000

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: How a mountain rescue team fight its way through appalling conditions to rescue a walker on Snowdon. Plus, the mountain biker who crash-landed after a jump, leading his friends to believe he's broken his neck. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt are in Nottinghamshire to help two teams look for items of value at the Newark Antiques and Collectable fair. Presenter Charlie Ross hits the fair to see if anyone can help him with a mystery object and later in the show the team heads to Derby for the highs and lows of the auction

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Billie, Seb, Victoria and Joshua vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges artists to make something amazing for the homes of people they've never met. In Manchester, the buyers want to celebrate the day their lives changed for ever -10 years ago when they won the National Lottery. Will it be a molten glass specialist, an Icelandic abstract artist or a self-taught metal sculptor who fulfils their brief?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman visits Norfolk to help a father and daughter who hope their £700,000 budget will provide them with a large equestrian property and annexe to house their family of four, along with their horse. Nicki also heads to the Norfolk Broads and learns about the restoration efforts of the Norfolk Wherry Trust before taking to the water to sail one of these traditional wooden boats

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is at Witley Recycling Centre, salvaging three unwanted gems so they can shine again. A broken display cabinet finds its way to furniture artist Chloe Kempster, designer Daniel Heath needs to set a 1970s dressing table back on the right path, and Sarah tries to help a chandelier gleam again

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: For their last outing, Arusha Irvine and David Harper enjoy a whistle-stop tour of the Lake District, battling each other - and the elements - in a sporty classic car. David's hunt for antiques unearths a secret map issued by the RAF, but with time ticking before the final showdown Roo gambles on a Georgian long case clock. The duo then head to North Shields for the auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker looks back at highlights of the 50th series of the quiz show so far, featuring team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell alongside guests including Ugo Monye, Jade Jones, Stuart Pearce, Osi Umenyiora and Sam Quek

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Karen snaps when offered a free meal from the cafe, sick of everyone judging her and her family. Nancy reluctantly agrees to babysit for Sharon, but while looking after Albie she feels unwell and only just manages to put the child back in his playpen before collapsing to the ground with an epileptic seizure

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Actors and comedians Liz Carr and Miles Jupp, actor and writer Samson Kayo and broadcaster Anneka Rice.join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts the satirical quiz, with panellists Lemn Sissay and Jo Brand joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Keen to demonstrate the extent of his physical fitness, Alan embarks on a high-octane SAS training mission. Resident drinks expert Rosie Whitter joins the team in the This Time studio. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu, Tim Key and Rosie Cavaliero

programme.title: Long Shot (2019)
programme.desc: Out-of-work journalist Fred Flarsky secures employment writing speeches for glamorous US Secretary of State Charlotte Field, who is shortly to run for office as the first female president. Opposites attract and a very unlikely romance develops during an international campaign to champion environmental issues. Comedy drama, starring Charlize Theron and Seth Rogen

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Glynn Purnell and Saliha Mahmood-Ahmed, plus special guest Jay Blades, and drinks expert Helen McGinn picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain takes a culinary road trip across Yorkshire, meeting fisherman Frank Powell near Bridlington and using some of his catch to cook baked sea trout with sun-dried tomatoes and lemons. She then heads to Huddersfield to sample local halloumi made by Syrian refugee Razan Alsous, creating watermelon and halloumi skewers. At home, Nadiya makes rhubarb and custard ice-cream sandwiches and Yorkshire puddings filled with strawberry jam and custard

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Chelsea v Leicester City in the FA Cup final

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: Match of Their Day

programme.title: FA Cup: The Road to Wembley
programme.desc: A look at the biggest stories of this season's FA Cup ahead of this weekend's final between Chelsea and Leicester City at Wembley Stadium

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Leicester City (Kick-off 5.15pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the showpiece match at Wembley Stadium. The Blues have won this competition on eight occasions, with six of those triumphs coming since the turn of the Millennium, most recently in 2018 when they overcame Manchester United 1-0 in the final. In contrast, the Foxes have never lifted the trophy and this is the first time they have played in the final since 1969. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Ian Wright

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Heather Small, as they assist Caitlin and Kristen from Warrington in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a singing wrestler, a model and a dentist

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as Chris Hollins, Dallas Campbell, Phil Daniels, Levi Roots, Lou Sanders, Jayde Adams, Lucy Porter and Dave Johns take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Rash is thrown into the deep end when Ethan signs him up for the finance meeting and his day gets even more complicated when an old friend arrives in the ED. Iain and Faith bond as they treat a dementia patient and her adoring husband, and Tina reminisces on her grief over Nate

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Southampton v Fulham at St Mary's Stadium and Brighton & Hove Albion v West Ham United at Amex Stadium. Fulham and Brighton are still battling it out to avoid relegation to the Championship, while the Hammers were looking to move a step closer to securing a place in Europe. Plus, Burnley v Leeds United and the Friday night contest between Newcastle United and Manchester City

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Leicester City. Gabby Logan presents action from the final at Wembley Stadium, as the sides battled it out for the prestigious trophy. The Blues secured their place in the showpiece match with a 1-0 last-four victory over Manchester City, while Leicester overcame Southampton by the same scoreline

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Micah Richards and Alan Shearer to count down 10 of the most famous showpiece matches in the world's oldest domestic cup competition

programme.title: X+Y (2014)
programme.desc: A specially awkward teenager finds it difficult to communicate with people, but finds comfort in his passion for numbers. However, while training to take part in the forthcoming International Mathematical Olympiad, he finds love with a kindred spirit. Comedy drama, starring Asa Butterfield, Rafe Spall and Sally Hawkins

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Southampton v Fulham at St Mary's Stadium and Brighton & Hove Albion v West Ham United at Amex Stadium. Fulham and Brighton are still battling it out to avoid relegation to the Championship, while the Hammers were looking to move a step closer to securing a place in Europe. Plus, Burnley v Leeds United and the Friday night contest between Newcastle United and Manchester City

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Leicester City. Gabby Logan presents action from the final at Wembley Stadium, as the sides battled it out for the prestigious trophy. The Blues secured their place in the showpiece match with a 1-0 last-four victory over Manchester City, while Leicester overcame Southampton by the same scoreline

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: Rev Kate Bottley hears from people who have given up their time to help others, including the UK's youngest chief executive and a couple who offered their home to a Syrian refugee

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Santiago
programme.desc: The seven celebrities continue their trek along the Camino de Santiago pilgrimage to their destination of the Cathedral of Santiago de Compostela. The Rev Kate Bottley discovers a fountain of free-flowing wine, while Ed Byrne meets a man who believes money is the root of all evil. As the group bonds strengthen, JJ Chalmers and Neil Morrissey open up about traumatic experiences that have shaped their lives, while Debbie McGee leaves a stone bearing the name of her late husband, Paul Daniels, at the foot of the cathedral's famous Iron Cross. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Raj Bisram help two teams at Southwell racecourse in Nottinghamshire make a profit at auction. Hosted by Anita Manning

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones explores the Cathedral town of Exeter and, in Dementia Action Week, YolanDa Brown meets one local woman helping people with dementia to remain active

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers have their say on the BBC's output and there are behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple in the Welsh county of Ceredigion, looking to buy a country home with the potential for a spiritual retreat on a budget of £355,000

programme.title: Cars 3 (2017)
programme.desc: Constantly outshined and outraced by a fleet of hi-tech rookies, former champ Lightning McQueen must find a new way to win. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Owen Wilson, Cristela Alonzo and Chris Cooper

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates footage of a lion pride on Kenya's Masai Mara that has reached a dangerous and unusual turning point where the future of the group hangs in the balance. The adult males that had defended the pride have all deserted the group, leaving two remaining adult females to protect their eight cubs from a wide range of threats, including rival prides, herds of buffalo and the constant menace of marauding hyenas. However, the greatest threat of all comes when the lions encounter humans

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith head to the Peak District to celebrate the 70th anniversary of the UK's first National Park. Matt joins a pioneering scheme to restore ruined farm buildings and meets 'legend' of the Peak District Gordon Miller, one of the area's early rangers and a last link with those who brought about the National Park. Charlotte channels her inner artist to help to create a giant anniversary art mural and records the special sounds of the Park's waterways, while Joe Crowley investigates new pollution laws causing controversy in the countryside

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The Roadshow is at Stonor Park in Oxfordshire, where finds include a rare group of Indian medals, Florence Nightingale's toolkit and the story of a remarkable female pilot from the Second World War. There is also an intriguing painting by an unknown artist, some computer generated art with a surprisingly high value and a collection of rare pearls from around the world

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne is concerned when a toddler displays troubling symptoms, while an overly protective mother struggles to cope with her daughter's teenage pregnancy and is insistent on keeping it low key, much to the frustration of new Pupil Midwife Nancy

programme.title: The Pursuit of Love
programme.desc: Fanny embraces motherhood and tries to live up to society's image of a good wife, while Linda throws herself into a lifestyle of all-night parties as her marriage to Tony almost immediately falls apart. When she is pulled away by an enigmatic stranger, she sees the chance to make a drastic change and start a whole new life

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including West Bromwich Albion v Liverpool at The Hawthorns. Sam Allardyce's men held the Reds to a 1-1 draw when the sides met at Anfield in December in what was the start of a downturn in form for Liverpool. Plus, Everton v Sheffield United, Crystal Palace v Aston Villa and Tottenham Hotspur v Wolverhampton Wanderers

programme.title: Once Were Lions
programme.desc: Documentary following the rugby league Great Britain Lions' tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea, as they fight for their sporting future

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: JJ Chalmers teams up with upcycler Sarah Peterson and lighting expert Duncan McKean to revive a pair of side tables, a rattan chair and some vintage lawnmower buckets

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin heads to his old stomping ground, near Stratford-upon-Avon, to see an intriguing lot consisting of 16 garages. Tommy Walsh is in Wolverhampton looking at a three-bed detached property, and Martel Martel is in Sunderland where a four bed mid-terrace has a bit of a damp problem

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Vintage bike restorer Tim Gunn brings all his skill to bear on a wartime bicycle once owned and used by a Bletchley Park code breaker. Horologist Steve Fletcher recruits fellow expert and sister Suzie to work on a neglected and battered old Victorian firefighter's helmet. A threadbare teddy on wheels is also restored

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc hosts a game show in which pairs of contestants go head to head attempting to crack a series of passwords, in a bid to win £2,500

programme.title: Good Morning Dagenham
programme.desc: The area surrounding the wasteland upon which the market sits is being developed into attractive and desirable flats. Dagenham is being gentrified and that's given market boss Frank an idea - can they draw in a new, well-heeled sort of customer by going upmarket? Narrated by Larry Lamb

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: The consequences of Henry's accident brings soul searching for some. Meanwhile, Anna arrives back in Sydney with some exciting news from Hollywood

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The contestants are given just three hours to produce their ultimate children's birthday cake - and as if that were not tough enough, Mary Berry reminds them she knows a thing or two about baking. This is followed by Chris Bavin's challenge to rustle something up from leftovers, and he makes the task twice as hard by stripping back the contents of the larders. The judges then decide who will have to compete in Angela Hartnett's eliminator, by the end of which another contestant will be hanging up their apron. Claudia Winkleman hosts

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary revisiting the pride of African lions featured in Africa's Giant Killers: Natural World, which detailed a clash between drought-weakened elephants and hungry big cats in the northern stretches of the Kalahari Desert in Botswana. This follow-up rejoins the pride at the start of the rainy season, when the departure of the elephants brings a whole host of new problems for the lions. The males begin to turn against each other, and one inexperienced lioness places her cubs in mortal danger as a rival group challenges the area's resident lions. Wild fires caused by lightning devastate the landscape, and as the dust begins to settle, the pride must once again face off with the elephants or risk starvation. Narrated by Imogen Stubbs

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts Catherine Southon and Mark Stacey value items and collectibles brought in by the public to Lulworth Castle in Dorset. Plus, presenter Paul Martin explores the turbulent history of the Weld family and the anti-Catholic persecution they were subject to for more than 300 years

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Chizzy Akudolu, Miles Jupp, Amol Rajan and Ellie Taylor test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games. Their scores are tallied across the week resulting in an overall champion

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In the elegant coastal town of Aberystwyth, Michael Portillo visits the National Library of Wales, set high on a hill with views over Cardigan Bay. His tour of North Wales draws to a close in style at the birthplace of the nation's first classical music festival, Gregynog Hall, near Newtown

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas and the team take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: Mary Beard and guests discuss how we remember - not just as individuals, but as a society and a nation. She is joined by architect David Adjaye, writer Elif Shafak, artists Edmund de Waal and Cornelia Parker, poet Lemn Sissay, and actor Jane Horrocks

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The Porthcawl crew have just seconds to spare when two teenagers on holiday get caught on a rocky outcrop by a large swell and a rising spring tide, while in East Yorkshire, the Flamborough crew have to pluck a man from danger after he jumps off a 100 foot cliff for a dare. In Devon, the seas are often crowded with sailing craft of all shapes and sizes and when two collide, the Torbay lifeboat crew are called out to provide emergency medical assistance for a potentially life-changing injury

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from the south west of England go head to head in the last of the regional finals as they cook their six-course menus again. The judges are food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and food creative Rachel Khoo, as well as a guest judge, inventor Colin Furze. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don plants out his tomatoes and shows viewers what we can feed our plants. Arit Anderson looks into the use of peat in the horticultural industry, and Sue Kent reveals the progress on her allotment

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: The pianist welcomes Noel Gallagher, who looks back at 10 years of his band Noel Gallagher's High Flying Birds. Plus, Walsall born singer-songwriter Jorja Smith, and Ohio duo the Black Keys also join Jools at his South London recording studio

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events

programme.title: Manchester by the Sea (2016)
programme.desc: An irritable loner is forced to return to the coastal town where he grew up following the sudden death of his brother. He is appointed guardian of his teenage nephew, and as he struggles to cope with his newfound responsibilities, he also finds himself confronted with a past tragedy. Drama, starring an Oscar-winning Casey Affleck, with Michelle Williams and Kyle Chandler

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: The Viper pilots work with the Cylon rebels to draw up a battle plan to strike at the heart of the enemy's powers by attacking the Resurrection Hub. Edward James Olmos, Katee Sackhoff and Jamie Bamber star

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: The humans' truce with the Cylon rebels backfires when they hold the President hostage in an attempt to lure the final five machines out of hiding. Meanwhile, it appears that Starbuck may be confirming the Hybrid's prophecy by leading the fleet into danger

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Behind the scenes with Kent police tackling the drugs trade, and the 'county lines' set up to distribute narcotics bought in London across the county - often using children and vulnerable people as carriers. The programme examines how the illegal drugs trade has continued to flourish despite the limitations placed on movement during lockdown

programme.title: Saved By a Stranger
programme.desc: The stories of ordinary people caught up in the biggest events in living memory, and the person who changed their lives. It begins with Karl, a trainee clinical psychologist who was a passenger on the first carriage of the Piccadilly Line London Underground train - one of the four targets of the 7/7 bombings. After the bomb exploded, Karl was comforted by a woman who held his hand in the smoke-filled carriage as he feared for his life. Cameras also follow Emina, an NHS researcher living in Nottingham. She and her younger sister Edina, who was diagnosed with Down Syndrome as a baby, were born in Sarajevo. Edina is searching for the doctor who helped her family evacuate to safety, and gave Edina the chance of a better life. Anita Rani presents

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Naomi Wilkinson plays clips featuring an angry, dinosaur-like creature on the warpath and a lemming terrorising a cat

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Team captains Maya and Charlie are joined by comedians Helen Bauer and Maisie Adams, TV presenter David Morgan and Dr Ranj Singh

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Part two of two. With the help of all on-screen designers and the Blue Peter audience, efforts to build an amazing den for the children and their siblings to enjoy continue apace at the Claire House Children's Hospice in the Wirral

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The winner of the gaming competition is revealed

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and the team continue to island-hop across the Southern Ocean, beginning in the Falkland Islands where they encounter the rockhopper penguin and one of the area's fiercest underwater predators. They then endure four days of sailing through some of the roughest seas on Earth as they travel to the remote Bird Island off South Georgia, where they are met by hundreds of Antarctic fur seals and a wandering albatross

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes' series exploring the lives and teachings of influential ancient philosophers draws to a close with a profile of Confucius, who is credited as the first Chinese thinker to take a systematic philosophical approach to issues of social, political and ethical importance. Born during the chaotic Spring and Autumn period of Chinese history, Confucius believed that harmony could be promoted and achieved through the examples of sage rulers. He was also a innovative teacher, and tried to instill his principles of moral excellence in China's elite, but died without seeing his vision for his nation come to pass. However, his philosophies would eventually form the bedrock of Chinese culture, with many of his principles of tradition surviving to this day

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates the story of some of New Zealand's wildest places, home to hyper-intelligent parrots, snails with teeth, and constellations of glow-worms. In these often inhospitable locations, any creature hoping to survive must resort to radical techniques, none more so than on the South Island, which lies toward Antarctica and is home to the rapidly rising peaks of the Southern Alps

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: BBC wildlife presenters and crew take a trip through the natural history archives, beginning by sharing some of their most memorable animal encounters across Africa. Gordon Buchanan reminisces about the start of his career when he was 17, Chris Packham looks back on of his adventures in Kenya, and Steve Backshall shares a story about diving with Nile crocodiles in the Okavango delta of Botswana

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The regional heats conclude with the south west of England, as chefs Nick Beardshaw, Jude Kereama, Elly Wentworth, Nat Tallents compete with canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by British invention and innovation. The chefs draw upon their region's innovative past and present, with recipes inspired by paleontology and the many dinosaur discoveries on the Dorset coast, as well as its strong aeronautical heritage and current space research. At the end of the episode, one of the four must leave the competition

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs are halfway through their heat, and each wants to represent the South West at a banquet celebrating British invention and innovation. There's a surprise new veteran taking over from Angela Hartnett, who judged the previous round - the series' most successful competitor, Richard Corrigan. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Doris Day: Virgin Territory
programme.desc: A profile of the American singer, actress and animal rights activist, featuring contributions by James Garner, Richard Carpenter, Terence Davies and Don Pippin. The programme delves into the troubled private life and career highs and lows of the star of such films as Pillow Talk, Calamity Jane, That Touch of Mink, The Thrill of It All and Caprice. Narrated by Michael Brandon

programme.title: Calamity Jane (1953)
programme.desc: Brash tomboy gunslinger Jane goes on a mission to save her home town's saloon from financial ruin by bringing in a famous stage star to perform. This ladylike entertainer befriends Jane and shows her how to use her womanly wiles to attract a handsome cavalry officer. Unfortunately, he falls for the songstress - who is not the real professional she pretends to be, but her maid. Musical comedy Western, with Doris Day, Howard Keel, Allyn McLerie and Philip Carey

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Elizabeth Talbot and Nick Davies examine the best antiques at Milestones Museum in Basingstoke, and Paul Martin takes a tour round a modern cruise ship to find out about the history of the Southampton cruise liners

programme.title: All That Glitters: Britain's Next Jewellery Star
programme.desc: Katherine Ryan hosts as the last four contestants have to make a cuff bracelet, then attempt to create an engagement ring for couple James and Clare, before judges Shaun Leane and Solange Azagury-Partridge decide who to put through to the final

programme.title: Emin/Munch: Between the Clock and the Bed
programme.desc: This winter sees the opening of The Loneliness of the Soul, a new exhibition at London's Royal Academy, showcasing the work of Tracey Emin, alongside paintings by Edvard Munch. Munch's work has long inspired Emin, from her early video piece Homage to Edvard Munch and All My Dead Children to her monumental new nine-metre high bronze sculpture, The Mother, set to be installed outside Oslo's new Munch Museum next spring. This documentary charts the affinities between Munch's art and Emin's, exploring the ways that this godfather of expressionism resonates with one of Britain's pre-eminent artists

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of a family-run roadside recovery business in the Highlands, as they rescue stranded motorists on some of the most remote roads in Britain

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Paul Murton travels from the shores of Loch Hope to the glorious and lonely expanse of Sandwood Bay in the far north-west of the country. Along the way he encounters a Pictish king and an ancient Broch, discovers wartime secrets in Loch Eriboll and a hidden monument to a naval disaster

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Sgt Wilson struggles to concentrate on his Home Guard duties when he has a personal problem to contend with. Classic comedy, starring John Le Mesurier and Arthur Lowe

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Another chance to see Steve Wright present a special edition first broadcast in 2000 to mark what would have been the former Beatle's 60th year. Featured songs include Imagine, Jealous Guy, Instant Karma, Mind Games and Stand By Me, and the programme includes an interview with Yoko Ono

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: A journey through the musician's life in 10 defining photos, with their secrets revealed by those who were there, and those who knew the musician best. The pictures show Lennon's lifelong search to find his place in the world, documenting his disaffection with living the life of a Beatle and his relationship with the woman who became the love of his life, the avant-garde artist Yoko Ono

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: The story of John Lennon and Yoko Ono's move to New York City in 1971, as the singer sought to escape the mayhem of the Beatles era and focus on his private life. Alan Yentob charts the ups and downs the couple faced - including their battle against the immigration services and Lennon's infamous 'lost weekend', when he left Yoko for their personal assistant and disappeared to LA for more than a year. The film features interviews with Ono, photographer Bob Gruen and Elton John, as well as studio recordings from the Double Fantasy sessions, concert out-takes and home-movie clips

programme.title: Source Code (2011)
programme.desc: A military pilot is the subject of a top-secret experiment that transfers his mind into the body of a man killed in a terrorist bombing on a train, but only for the last eight minutes of the dead man's life. His mission is to use that time to find out who is responsible and if they intend to strike again. Sci-fi thriller, starring Jake Gyllenhaal, Michelle Monaghan, Vera Farmiga and Jeffrey Wright

programme.title: Teddy Pendergrass: If You Don't Know Me
programme.desc: Documentary about the life, music and towering achievements of soul singer Teddy Pendergrass, from his origins and early life in Philadelphia. The film charts his early successes with Harold Melvin & the Blue Notes and his solo superstardom and sex symbol status, before moving on to his later life and continued success after overcoming the car crash that left him paralysed

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don plants out his tomatoes and shows viewers what they can feed their plants. Arit Anderson looks into the use of peat in the horticultural industry, and Sue Kent reveals the progress on her allotment

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison explore Cranborne Chase, help tend to Britain's biggest modern-day maze, and meet farmers who are working together to encourage wildlife on their land. Steve Brown goes stargazing, Tom Heap reports on the possible environmental costs of a new space race launched from rural sites, and Adam Henson takes his rare breed Suffolk Punch to the maternity unit

programme.title: Being
programme.desc: Documentary highlighting how British Jews from across the faith celebrate life's big milestones, from birth to coming of age, marriage to the end of life. A 12-year-old prepares for his bar mitzvah, a couple marry in Spain, a modern ceremony they have adapted according to their beliefs, and at Bushey Cemetery in Hertfordshire, a woman describes her role as a volunteer who does the tahara, the ritual washing of the deceased before burial

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond and Nick Nairn cook up some wonderful local produce on the Black Isle. Arlene Stuart investigates the welfare issues associated with fast growing chickens, while Euan McIlwraith continues his series about his favourite birds, focusing on the fulmar. The programme also catches up with Polly Pullar, the conservationist who rehabilitates an array of birds and beasties

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Brian Cunningham reviews the success of Beechgrove's box-hedging alternatives, while he and Mairi Rattray also share topiary tips. Plus, catching up with the Seil Community Garden, which Beechgrove helped create in 2009.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights from throughout the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: The Great Northern Garden Build
programme.desc: Documentary following the creation of RHS Garden Bridgewater in Salford, one of the most ambitious and challenging gardening projects the UK has ever seen. The programme goes behind the scenes to meet the team of designers, engineers and gardeners taking on this colossal construction project on the site of a former grand Victorian estate in Greater Manchester

programme.title: Rick Stein's India
programme.desc: The chef arrives in the state of Rajasthan, a land of palaces and hilltop forts, where he cooks a biryani fit for a royal banquet. He then heads north to the mountainous region of Himachal Pradesh, and joins local nobility at a gathering in their home village and has an audience with the Dalai Lama

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from the south west of England go head to head in the last of the regional finals as they cook their six-course menus again. The judges are food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and food creative Rachel Khoo, as well as a guest judge, inventor Colin Furze. Andi Oliver hosts

programme.title: Live Diving
programme.desc: Live coverage of the final day of diving from the European Aquatics Championships in Budapest

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Celia Imrie looks at the careers of leading British actresses, including Emma Thompson, Diana Rigg, Julie Walters, Elizabeth Taylor, Maggie Smith, Judi Dench and Helen Mirren

programme.title: Tea with Mussolini (1999)
programme.desc: Eccentric English and American women living in Florence during the tense pre-war years of the 1930s experience the ups and downs of parenthood when they collectively adopt a local boy disowned by his father. However, as international hostilities break out, their happy existence comes under threat. Period comedy drama, starring Maggie Smith, Judi Dench, Joan Plowright, Cher, Lily Tomlin and Baird Wallace

programme.title: Live Diving
programme.desc: Further live coverage of the final day of diving from the European Aquatics Championships in Budapest, featuring the women's 3 metre synchro and the men's 10 metre platform finals

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: BBC wildlife presenters reflect on their visits to the Arctic, with Liz Bonnin looking back on her time helping a team of scientists locate a population of rare Siberian tigers. Gordon Buchanan recounting the time he went to Ellesmere Island in Canada in search of wild wolves that had never seen humans before, and Chris Packham remembers the time he ventured to Greenland on an expedition to uncover the secret life of an iceberg

programme.title: Gods of Snooker
programme.desc: A look at the emergence of Steve Davis and the rise of promoter Barry Hearn's Matchroom group of players, which brought lucrative sponsorships into the game. By the mid-1980s, snooker was at the peak of its powers and in 1985 nearly 20 million people tuned in to see Steve Davis play Dennis Taylor in the World Championship final

programme.title: Davis v Taylor: The '85 Black Ball Final
programme.desc: Colin Murray presents a look back at the final of the 1985 World Snooker Championship between Dennis Taylor and Steve Davis. The match finished after midnight, but kept an estimated 18.5 million viewers engrossed until the title was decided on the final black of the 35th frame. With contributions from Barry McGuigan, Ted Lowe, Stephen Hendry and Barry Hearn

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Franklin's troubles are affecting Cissy and Alton in their home, while Gustavo has sworn revenge against Fuentes, but his uncompromising methods jeopardise Teddy's operation

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Even though he is now out on his own, Franklin's problems keep landing on Leon's doorstep, and he decides to handle it his own way. Teddy deals with the repercussions of his diversion in Mexico with Gustavo, while Irene is getting closer to something big

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a panel of politicians and other guests facing questions from an audience made up of members of the general public

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Donna leads the Holby nursing staff in protest - will Hanssen be moved by their cause or are things about to get much worse for them? Lucky realises that Kian lied to her about where Andrei was staying, while an accidental encounter between Josh and Hanssen threatens to spill both of their secrets

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: How Pop Songs Work
programme.desc: Charles Hazlewood explores what makes a good pop single, breaking the songs down into six key areas - melody, song structure, lyrics, production arrangement and performance - before looking at successful songs in the genre. The conductor uses successful albums from the genre including John Lennon's Imagine and Amy Winehouse's Back to Black to examine these categories. Contributors include songwriter Guy Chambers, ABC singer Martin Fry and producers Steve Levine and Richard Niles

programme.title: Ultimate Cover Versions at the BBC
programme.desc: The pop cover has proved a remarkably imaginative and durable form, and this 60-year compilation tracks this pop alchemy at its finest and most intriguing through material sought from the Corporation's archive. Footage includes artists as diverse as the Moody Blues, Soft Cell, Alexandra Burke, Mariah Carey and UB40 scoring chart hits that are, in some cases, perhaps even better than the original

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier and Anthea Turner host the edition first aired on Christmas Day, with a look back at 1990's biggest chart hits featuring original performances by Status Quo, Londonbeat, Adamski, Kylie Minogue, Bombalurina, the Beautiful South, Beats International featuring Lindy Layton, Elton John and Kim Appleby

programme.title: The Old Grey Whistle Test Story
programme.desc: Jo Brand narrates a history of the popular music show which ran from 1971 to 1987. The documentary examines the music performed on the show, its rivals on other networks and exactly why presenter Bob Harris whispered. Featuring contributions by Jarvis Cocker, Annie Nightingale and Jools Holland

programme.title: The Old Grey Whistle Test
programme.desc: A musical journey through the decade, featuring performances on the programme by Elton John, David Bowie, Curtis Mayfield, Steppenwolf, New York Dolls, Roxy Music, Patti Smith, Blondie, Iggy Pop and the Jam

programme.title: How Pop Songs Work
programme.desc: Charles Hazlewood explores what makes a good pop single, breaking the songs down into six key areas - melody, song structure, lyrics, production arrangement and performance - before looking at successful songs in the genre. The conductor uses successful albums from the genre including John Lennon's Imagine and Amy Winehouse's Back to Black to examine these categories. programme.title: Ultimate Cover Versions at the BBC
programme.desc: The pop cover has proved a remarkably imaginative and durable form, and this 60-year compilation tracks this pop alchemy at its finest and most intriguing through material sought from the Corporation's archive. Footage includes artists as diverse as the Moody Blues, Soft Cell, Alexandra Burke, Mariah Carey and UB40 scoring chart hits that are, in some cases, perhaps even better than the original

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Mark Goodier and Anthea Turner host the edition first aired on Christmas Day, with a look back at 1990's biggest chart hits featuring original performances by Status Quo, Londonbeat, Adamski, Kylie Minogue, Bombalurina, the Beautiful South, Beats International featuring Lindy Layton, Elton John and Kim Appleby

programme.title: Francesco's Venice
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto studies the city during the 18th century, and its fascination with art and sex. Casanova became an overnight celebrity and courtesans openly plied their trade, while great art was produced by Canaletto and Canova, and music by Vivaldi. But the hedonism could not go on indefinitely as first syphilis then the might of Napoleon's armies took their toll upon the city

programme.title: Michael Palin's New Europe
programme.desc: The presenter joins the mystical White Brotherhood sect in Bulgaria's Rila Mountains and travels to Sofia, where he meets a transvestite singer who has become an unlikely Balkan superstar. He then heads to the Asian side of Istanbul, where he learns belly dancing and discovers how many people in the country now consider themselves to be European. Michael also marvels at a camel-wrestling festival and visits a converted rock monastery

programme.title: Blinded: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: The police launch a huge search for William, suspecting he's been taken by the killer, while Peter Vinge tries to celebrate Johannes's birthday without Masja. In Danish

programme.title: Blinded: Those Who Kill
programme.desc: As the police prepare a public appeal to find new leads on the killer, Alice offers a reward on social media for information. Louise's boyfriend comes to visit her. In Danish

programme.title: Catching History's Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: An insight into the development of forensic science as a way of catching murderers over the past 200 years, charting the scientific breakthroughs that have changed the course of justice. Surgeon and writer Gabriel Weston begins by looking at the difficulty of identifying the body in a murder case, from charred bones to bodies completely dissolved in acid. She focuses on the use of teeth and bite marks to identify a victim or killer, traces the uses of insects to pinpoint the time of death, and meets the geneticist who pioneered the technique of DNA profiling

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: Meyer begins to feel in charge of the investigation, but Lund unexpectedly returns and decides to launch a search for Theis Larsen and prime murder suspect Rama following their disappearance. Meanwhile, Hartmann faces pressure from inside his party and is torn between his advisers and his own sense of morality, while Lund's time in Copenhagen only serves to increase her personal problems. Danish drama, starring Sofie Grabol, Soren Malling and Lars Mikkelsen

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: Lund and Meyer are helped by the local imam in their search for the hiding place of Rama's collaborator, and Hartmann is given an ultimatum by his party due to his handling of the scandal. Meanwhile, Theis and Pernille suspect the investigation may be coming to a close, only for it to take an unexpected turn. Danish drama, starring Sofie Grabol, Soren Malling and Lars Mikkelsen

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: Theis has disappeared with murder suspect Rama and seems more prepared than ever to take the law into his own hands. Lund and Meyer are given 24 hours to find a new lead or they will be thrown off the case, and the political landscape undergoes radical changes at the town hall. Meanwhile, Pernille has to cope with being on her own and manage the family business. Danish drama, starring Sofie Grabol and Ann Eleonora Jorgensen

programme.title: The Killing
programme.desc: Lund and Meyer pursue a new lead, but are taken aback when their boss intervenes, while Hartmann encounters fierce resistance as he tries to establish a new alliance at the town hall. Theis still awaits release in custody and Pernille begins to suspect that someone close to her may be hiding something. Danish drama, starring Sofie Grabol and Lars Mikkelsen

programme.title: Revolution and Romance: Musical Masters of the 19th Century
programme.desc: Suzy Klein's odyssey through the history of music in the 19th century concludes, turning the spotlight on later years, when technological revolutions seemed to make the world a smaller, more accessible place. Focusing on the Paris Exposition of 1889, a cultural showcase celebrating the centenary of the French Revoution, she reveals how composers such as Claude Debussy were exposed to the sounds of the East - immediately breaking Wagner's stranglehold of influence over Western music. She also examines the invention of saxophone, and makes her own recording on a vintage phonograph - an early precursor of vinyl records, cassette tapes and CDs

programme.title: Sound Waves: The Symphony of Physics
programme.desc: Helen Czerski investigates the science behind familiar sounds as well as those humans cannot hear. At the Palace of Westminster, she teams up with scientists to carry out measurements using state-of-the-art lasers to reveal how Big Ben vibrates to create pressure waves in the air at particular frequencies. She also explores the science of the voice, inviting soprano Lesley Garrett to sing I Dreamed A Dream inside an MRI scanner to reveal how her vocal tract acts as a resonator, amplifying and shaping the sound from her larynx

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: Docudrama portrait of Delia Derbyshire, the electronic sound pioneer behind the Doctor Who theme tune, exploring the idea that this extraordinary composer herself lived outside of time and space. Delia's story is told through two archives: the first, a collection of lost works, 267 reels of quarter-inch magnetic tape recordings of Delia's work found in her attic after her death, the other, her school books, paintings and keepsakes, discovered in her childhood bedroom

programme.title: BBC Proms 2018
programme.desc: From London's Royal Albert Hall, ground-breaking British composer Anna Meredith presents this tribute to the godmothers of electronica, the pioneers of the BBC Radiophonic Workshop. The celebration begins with music by Delia Derbyshire - most famously remembered for bringing the world the Doctor Who theme in its full electronic glory and finishes with the premiere of Daphne Oram's Still Point, pieced together from recently discovered archive material and performed by Shiva Feshareki on turntables. Music by artists including Laurie Spiegel, Chaines and Suzanne Ciani emphasise the power of this legacy, performed by the London Contemporary Orchestra under conductor Robert Ames.

programme.title: Dawn Chorus: The Sounds of Spring
programme.desc: Filmed without voiceover or music, this programme highlights the call of the birds at sunrise, as those living within Britain's habitats burst into song and reveal the natural splendour of their distinctive chorus

programme.title: Into the Wind
programme.desc: Writer and birdwatcher Tim Dee walks along the vast open flatlands of the Lincolnshire Wash, on a quest to capture the elusive sound of 'pure' wind. On his travels, he reflects on the landscape, walking, writing, birds and wind. From documentary film maker Richard Alwyn

programme.title: Sound Waves: The Symphony of Physics

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Emlyn, is a 13-year-old sheep farmer who lives in Wales. All year round, alongside his dog Patch, Emlyn is responsible for over a thousand sheep, helping them to deliver healthy baby lambs that will allow the family to survive. As a bitter winter hits the farm, hundreds of lambs' lives are at risk and Emlyn rushes to help them pull through. He's following in a long tradition - his family has been farming for more than 150 years - but tragedy struck when Emlyn was four years old when his father died. Now he takes responsibility for helping his mum to keep the farm going, which is crucial for the family's survival. Emlyn combines life as a secondary school student with the demands of a busy sheep farm. He's up at 6.30 every morning, touring the farm on his quadbike before breakfast, and then again for a few hours after school. There are always jobs to do from rescuing sheep that have escaped to spreading manure on the fields, fixing fences and feeding the sheep. As the year progresses Em

programme.desc: Family comedy adapted from David Walliams' children's book about 12-year-old Dennis, an ordinary boy who lives in an ordinary town - but somehow feels different from everyone else. A chance sighting in a fashion magazine introduces him to a whole new world of colour and creativity. But can a boy really wear a dress - and what will his headmaster, dad and friends in the football team think if they find out? Billy Kennedy stars as Dennis, with a supporting cast including Jennifer Saunders, Tim McInnerny, Meera Syal, Steve Speirs, Felicity Montagu, James Buckley and Walliams himself, with a cameo appearance by supermodel Kate Moss

programme.title: One Zoo Three
programme.desc: Temperatures rise as the zoo is hit by a heatwave. Many of the animals are well adapted to the sun, but those from cooler climates need extra attention. The boys devise a series of ingenious tricks to keep each animal cool, including ice baths, mist showers and giant ice lollies. It’s fish flavour for the penguins, lettuce lollies for Marion the tortoise and a huge blood lolly for the big cats. Meanwhile, the camels get their yearly comb-down, Tyler has to muck out the stinky Penguin pool and the boys play with an orphaned elephant in Uganda.

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Alara, Samaa, Reece, Eleanor, Ty’lana and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their little ones at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is are some scented lavender bags, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that are smelly. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate smelly objects and toys inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The children’s nursery rhyme Ring A Ring O' Roses features, as does a story involving Baby Bear, and viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring smelly things too. The Action Song gets everyone moving about before some calm time with sensory lights and the Goodbye Song.

programme.desc: Join nine-month-old Bonnie and Harry as they discover the world around them and become more expressive. Both children love playing with their families - laughing, smiling, clapping, playing peek-a-boo and making noise. Bonnie gets excited when her dad suggests a day out to the beach! When they get there, Bonnie is concentrating on making sandcastles before splashing her feet in the water, laughing and loving the sea. Harry and his family go on a bug hunt at a nature reserve, but what will they find? While the family enjoys a relaxing picnic, Harry tries a banana for the very first time. Bonnie is happily crawling along the beach finding seashells before mum lays out a picnic with yummy hummus and pitta bread. Before leaving, there is much laughter as they decide to try and completely cover dad with sand. After their picnic, Harry’s decides to join his brother in having his photo taken, and it’s big smiles all around!
