programme.desc: With Hodge’s radiator broken and no way to order another one, Brewster turns out to be the only one that can help by making a brave decision.

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bitz and Bob's junk stall sells out of everything, including Bob's robo-helmet! Bob is horrified

programme.title: The Furchester Hotel
programme.desc: A couple of very excited rabbits check into the hotel, and a famous singing fruit arrives to do a performance on the hotel rooftop

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: As Bluey and Bingo squabble over their magic xylophone (that has the power to freeze Dad in space and time) Dad seizes control and freezes Bluey, leaving Bingo as her only hope.

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bitz, Bob and Purl are in a race across the whole wide world to win the trophiest trophy ever, and they are joined by a new team member, Roly, a roller-skating baby dinosaur

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch visits Rome to race his Grimbots in the Colosseum. Can the Go Jetters stop him?

programme.title: Nick Cope's Popcast
programme.desc: Recycling gives Nick an idea for a Popcast Song about a rusty old robot

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Justin is in a singing mood and has been singing so loudly all day that he has lost his voice. Ever the caring friend, Robert tries various funny ways to help

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Andy travels to Japan to record a video of a male pufferfish building a nest in the sand

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk to a great place to play a train game. On the way they find a ten-mile-long snake, make friends with a woodlouse, listen to the wind blowing through willow trees and even stomp like giants!

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: George gets locked inside Mrs Hobbs' house and Waffle has a chance to prove himself to Mrs Hobbs. Can he rescue George?

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Mack tries to help Daisy by bringing Twinkle the pony to the vet's surgery. Twinkle escapes and ends up in the Big Hub - which doesn't please Moira at all

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Mummy and Dad go out for dinner and the twins are looked after by their new babysitter, Danielle

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Pippa is making myama, a chicken stew that uses tasty spring greens

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: When Gran Gran and JoJo find a family recipe for Great Gran Gran's signature Saint Lucian banana bread, they're excited to bake a batch of it! But there's one problem - cake mix has been spilt on the recipe, so they don't know how long to bake it for!

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: Salty and Jack discover that Angela the angler fish has been keeping a big secret

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: Storm Huntley meets some sheep dogs, as well as a young gardener at a local gardening project, while JB Gill learns all about tractors, before sharing a new Down on the Farm Spring poem

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Energetic animation for children aged two to five, following Duggee the dog and his Squirrel Club

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Energetic animation for children aged two to five, following Duggee the dog and his Squirrel Club

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The grimbots attach a bit of the Taj Mahal to GrimHQ. It remains to be seen whether the Go Jetters can fix the glitch

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: When Grandmaster Glitch makes popcorn in Mount Etna, Italy, he blocks the volcano and endangers farms on its slopes

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bob, or Big Bad Robo Wolf, showing just how bad he is, locks Ra-Purl-Zel in the castle tower. While Purl is busily knitting, Bitz and Sir Bevel try to engineer a way to rescue her

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: The Giggling Gurgle loves playing with B.O.T., bouncing on, and joyfully shaking the roving robot like a rattle! The Giggling Gurgle is easily upset and B.O.T. must find a way to cheer it up in order to complete the mission.

programme.title: Maddie, the Plants and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie on an adventure around the world of trees to see how we can help look after them and our planet. Discover how trees grow, how we can tell the age of trees and how the roots, leaves and bark work. Maddie shows how trees are home to lots of other plants, animals, birds and insects who live on and amongst them and have an important role in our ecosystem. We’ll discover a tree’s leaves make energy using sunlight, so they can keep growing. We learn that a tree is alive, it drinks and breathes and grows just like we do! And we’ll find out about deciduous trees that lose their leaves in winter and evergreens that keep their leaves all year round.

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Timmy makes a robot costume for himself out of cardboard boxes, with the help of Finlay

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: Bella is sad when Kobi reports that the bee hive is closing down. Mali suggests that the Apple Tree community looks after the bees in the hive, inspiring Grandma Zainab to get solving with a very practical solution

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: Topsy and Tim make Mummy a 'welcome home' surprise for the front of the house with Dad

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Connie and Ruby move into Maple Close with their dog, Jelly. Waffle is not allowed to go on a family camping trip but accidentally goes on a different adventure

programme.title: Ferne & Rory's Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Xiangfeng wants to know all about amazing ants! Ferne visits a leaf cutter ant colony at the zoo, while Rory and his son Sandy head into the forest, looking for wood ants. Plus, we find out how ants play an important role in the growing of plants and crops. They are amazing soil engineers!

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Meet Ted's Family. Ted has big plans for his sister Sophia's birthday and organises a fun family tea party

programme.title: The Toddler Club at Home
programme.desc: See how talking about things you do regularly helps your toddler feel safe and secure. Giovanna hosts from her own home. Join in with the Toddler Club Bop!

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Pink Bird runs wild in a department store, buying lots of beautiful things

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: Barry, Kari and Thingy try to decide what to play

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects associated with the colour yellow, from cheese to chicks, and we explore yellow in spring time. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour yellow.

programme.title: Maddie, the Home and You
programme.desc: Children's programme.

programme.title: GiggleQuiz
programme.desc: Arthur Sleep is joined by team captains Gail Force and Keith Fitt in this slapstick quiz show featuring guests and clips from Gigglebiz. Joining Gail and Keith for today’s GiggleQuiz are Wizard Tripwick and Captain Adorable. Round 1 features clips starring Professor Muddles showing off his recycling machine, and Dina Lady delivering one of her delicious cakes. Wizard Tripwick tries to guess the answer by using his wand. It all goes wrong in a very messy way, and a large cake ends up all over Gail. Wizard Tripwick receives a package from a magical pigeon, unwrapping it to reveal a brand new wand. Keen to test it out, he conjures Gail a cup of tea. Unfortunately, this new wand is more powerful than he thought! Round 2 is Nana’s Panto Showdown, and this time it features Nana’s frogs hopping into the pond to look for her socks. Round 3 features a clip starring DIY Dan making a garden for his gnomes. Wizard Tripwick is confident he’s got the hang of this new wand and offers help to everyone

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Every Friday is Baking Day in Struay and everyone creates their special treats - except Mrs McColl who is not a very good cook

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The youngster and her friend try to exercise - but the neighbours need their help

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: Charlie helps Lola stay clean and tidy for her first school photograph

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee wants his bike to go faster so Mo suggests she remove the stabilisers

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: When a radio falls out of the sky, Tiny, Small and Baby Soup Dragon are fascinated by the sounds that come from it

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Igglepiggle visits everyone in the garden and they give him something special

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Mark ROnson reads the story Rhyme Crime by Jon Burgerman

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford report on the online electronics retailer that has left dozens of customers in the lurch. Plus, Julia hears from the learners in lockdown who found self-education more expensive than they'd expected, explaining how to avoid falling foul of the small print

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development progress of terraced houses in Carlisle and Abbey Wood in London, as well as a seven-year project to renovate a large period house in Torquay

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley reports on the dedicated fraud-fighters pursuing the criminals who steal £40billion a year from the public purse. This edition features the amateur actress who put on a performance to evade justice for a showstopping £750,000 fraud and the school business manager who embezzled over £200,000

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: How a mountain rescue team fight its way through appalling conditions to rescue a walker on Snowdon. Plus, the mountain biker who crash-landed after a jump, leading his friends to believe he's broken his neck. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Billie, Seb, Victoria and Joshua vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges artists to make something amazing for the homes of people they've never met. In Manchester, the buyers want to celebrate the day their lives changed for ever -10 years ago when they won the National Lottery. Will it be a molten glass specialist, an Icelandic abstract artist or a self-taught metal sculptor who fulfils their brief?

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt are in Nottinghamshire to help two teams look for items of value at the Newark Antiques and Collectable fair. Presenter Charlie Ross hits the fair to see if anyone can help him with a mystery object and later in the show the team heads to Derby for the highs and lows of the auction

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is at Witley Recycling Centre, salvaging three unwanted gems so they can shine again. A broken display cabinet finds its way to furniture artist Chloe Kempster, designer Daniel Heath needs to set a 1970s dressing table back on the right path, and Sarah tries to help a chandelier gleam again

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: For their last outing, Arusha Irvine and David Harper enjoy a whistle-stop tour of the Lake District, battling each other - and the elements - in a sporty classic car. David's hunt for antiques unearths a secret map issued by the RAF, but with time ticking before the final showdown Roo gambles on a Georgian long case clock. The duo then head to North Shields for the auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Weatherman Walking: The Welsh Coast
programme.desc: Derek Brockway takes the circular route around the Great Orme on a trek from Deganwy to Llandudno, and as part of his journey braves his nerves alongside the elements to abseil down a section of the massive rock. Mo Jannah tries his hand at traditional mussel fishing, while Erin Lloyd-Jones explores the history of Llandudno Pier, meeting the designer's great, great grandson

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Karen snaps when offered a free meal from the cafe, sick of everyone judging her and her family. Nancy reluctantly agrees to babysit for Sharon, but while looking after Albie she feels unwell and only just manages to put the child back in his playpen before collapsing to the ground with an epileptic seizure

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker looks back at highlights of the 50th series of the quiz show so far, featuring team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell alongside guests including Ugo Monye, Jade Jones, Stuart Pearce, Osi Umenyiora and Sam Quek

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts the satirical quiz, with panellists Lemn Sissay and Jo Brand joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: This Time with Alan Partridge
programme.desc: Keen to demonstrate the extent of his physical fitness, Alan embarks on a high-octane SAS training mission. Resident drinks expert Rosie Whitter joins the team in the This Time studio. Comedy, starring Steve Coogan, Susannah Fielding, Felicity Montagu, Tim Key and Rosie Cavaliero

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Bossin' It
programme.desc: The entrepreneurial spirit among the young, spearheading an economic fightback, featuring high-end Cardiff nightclub owner Panikos Parpa who decided to 'cook his way' out of Covid by working with his business partners to create a pop-up 'restaurclub', where customers can eat, drink and dance at the same time. Plus, Toni Banter unveils her latest member of staff at Banter Cards - six-month-old Spencer, who now models Banter's brand-new baby range

programme.title: Long Shot (2019)
programme.desc: Out-of-work journalist Fred Flarsky secures employment writing speeches for glamorous US Secretary of State Charlotte Field, who is shortly to run for office as the first female president. Opposites attract and a very unlikely romance develops during an international campaign to champion environmental issues. Comedy drama, starring Charlize Theron and Seth Rogen

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Glynn Purnell and Saliha Mahmood-Ahmed, plus special guest Jay Blades, and drinks expert Helen McGinn picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain takes a culinary road trip across Yorkshire, meeting fisherman Frank Powell near Bridlington and using some of his catch to cook baked sea trout with sun-dried tomatoes and lemons. She then heads to Huddersfield to sample local halloumi made by Syrian refugee Razan Alsous, creating watermelon and halloumi skewers. At home, Nadiya makes rhubarb and custard ice-cream sandwiches and Yorkshire puddings filled with strawberry jam and custard

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Chelsea v Leicester City in the FA Cup final

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Thomas Plant help two teams find items at the Three Counties Showground in Malvern, Worcestershire, while presenter Eric Knowles oversees proceedings

programme.title: FA Cup Football
programme.desc: Classic moments involving this year's cup finalists, including Chelsea v Middlesbrough in 1997 and Leicester City v Manchester City in 1969

programme.title: Match of Their Day
programme.desc: Seven-time FA Cup-winner Ashley Cole joins Gary Lineker to look back at the best showpiece matches he played in for Arsenal and Chelsea

programme.title: FA Cup: The Road to Wembley
programme.desc: A look at the biggest stories of this season's FA Cup ahead of this weekend's final between Chelsea and Leicester City at Wembley Stadium

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Leicester City (Kick-off 5.15pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the showpiece match at Wembley Stadium. The Blues have won this competition on eight occasions, with six of those triumphs coming since the turn of the Millennium, most recently in 2018 when they overcame Manchester United 1-0 in the final. In contrast, the Foxes have never lifted the trophy and this is the first time they have played in the final since 1969. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Ian Wright

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Heather Small, as they assist Caitlin and Kristen from Warrington in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a singing wrestler, a model and a dentist

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as Chris Hollins, Dallas Campbell, Phil Daniels, Levi Roots, Lou Sanders, Jayde Adams, Lucy Porter and Dave Johns take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Rash is thrown into the deep end when Ethan signs him up for the finance meeting and his day gets even more complicated when an old friend arrives in the ED. Iain and Faith bond as they treat a dementia patient and her adoring husband, and Tina reminisces on her grief over Nate

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Southampton v Fulham at St Mary's Stadium and Brighton & Hove Albion v West Ham United at Amex Stadium. Fulham and Brighton are still battling it out to avoid relegation to the Championship, while the Hammers were looking to move a step closer to securing a place in Europe. Plus, Burnley v Leeds United and the Friday night contest between Newcastle United and Manchester City

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Leicester City. Gabby Logan presents action from the final at Wembley Stadium, as the sides battled it out for the prestigious trophy. The Blues secured their place in the showpiece match with a 1-0 last-four victory over Manchester City, while Leicester overcame Southampton by the same scoreline

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Micah Richards and Alan Shearer to count down 10 of the most famous showpiece matches in the world's oldest domestic cup competition

programme.title: X+Y (2014)
programme.desc: A specially awkward teenager finds it difficult to communicate with people, but finds comfort in his passion for numbers. However, while training to take part in the forthcoming International Mathematical Olympiad, he finds love with a kindred spirit. Comedy drama, starring Asa Butterfield, Rafe Spall and Sally Hawkins

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Southampton v Fulham at St Mary's Stadium and Brighton & Hove Albion v West Ham United at Amex Stadium. programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales. Presented by James Williams

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: Rev Kate Bottley hears from people who have given up their time to help others, including the UK's youngest chief executive and a couple who offered their home to a Syrian refugee

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Santiago
programme.desc: The seven celebrities continue their trek along the Camino de Santiago pilgrimage to their destination of the Cathedral of Santiago de Compostela. The Rev Kate Bottley discovers a fountain of free-flowing wine, while Ed Byrne meets a man who believes money is the root of all evil. As the group bonds strengthen, JJ Chalmers and Neil Morrissey open up about traumatic experiences that have shaped their lives, while Debbie McGee leaves a stone bearing the name of her late husband, Paul Daniels, at the foot of the cathedral's famous Iron Cross. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Raj Bisram help two teams at Southwell racecourse in Nottinghamshire make a profit at auction. Hosted by Anita Manning

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones explores the Cathedral town of Exeter and, in Dementia Action Week, YolanDa Brown meets one local woman helping people with dementia to remain active

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers have their say on the BBC's output and there are behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple in the Welsh county of Ceredigion, looking to buy a country home with the potential for a spiritual retreat on a budget of £355,000

programme.title: Cars 3 (2017)
programme.desc: Constantly outshined and outraced by a fleet of hi-tech rookies, former champ Lightning McQueen must find a new way to win. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Owen Wilson, Cristela Alonzo and Chris Cooper

programme.title: Iolo: The Last Wilderness of Wales
programme.desc: Iolo Williams tracks wildlife in the Cambrian mountains, the remote uplands that form the heart and spine of Wale. His first target is the elusive pine marten, finding one at night lurking in a conifer forest, and Iolo also looks for ospreys fishing on Llyn Clywedog

programme.title: Weatherman Walking: The Welsh Coast
programme.desc: Derek Brockway takes the circular route around the Great Orme on a trek from Deganwy to Llandudno, and as part of his journey braves his nerves alongside the elements to abseil down a section of the massive rock. Mo Jannah tries his hand at traditional mussel fishing, while Erin Lloyd-Jones explores the history of Llandudno Pier, meeting the designer's great, great grandson

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith head to the Peak District to celebrate the 70th anniversary of the UK's first National Park. Matt joins a pioneering scheme to restore ruined farm buildings and meets 'legend' of the Peak District Gordon Miller, one of the area's early rangers and a last link with those who brought about the National Park. Charlotte channels her inner artist to help to create a giant anniversary art mural and records the special sounds of the Park's waterways, while Joe Crowley investigates new pollution laws causing controversy in the countryside

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The Roadshow is at Stonor Park in Oxfordshire, where finds include a rare group of Indian medals, Florence Nightingale's toolkit and the story of a remarkable female pilot from the Second World War. There is also an intriguing painting by an unknown artist, some computer generated art with a surprisingly high value and a collection of rare pearls from around the world

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne is concerned when a toddler displays troubling symptoms, while an overly protective mother struggles to cope with her daughter's teenage pregnancy and is insistent on keeping it low key, much to the frustration of new Pupil Midwife Nancy

programme.title: The Pursuit of Love
programme.desc: Fanny embraces motherhood and tries to live up to society's image of a good wife, while Linda throws herself into a lifestyle of all-night parties as her marriage to Tony almost immediately falls apart. When she is pulled away by an enigmatic stranger, she sees the chance to make a drastic change and start a whole new life

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including West Bromwich Albion v Liverpool at The Hawthorns. Sam Allardyce's men held the Reds to a 1-1 draw when the sides met at Anfield in December in what was the start of a downturn in form for Liverpool. Plus, Everton v Sheffield United, Crystal Palace v Aston Villa and Tottenham Hotspur v Wolverhampton Wanderers

programme.desc: Documentary following the rugby league Great Britain Lions' tour of New Zealand and Papua New Guinea, as they fight for their sporting future

programme.desc: Out-of-work journalist Fred Flarsky secures employment writing speeches for glamorous US Secretary of State Charlotte Field, who is shortly to run for office as the first female president. Opposites attract and a very unlikely romance develops during an international campaign to champion environmental issues. Comedy drama, starring Charlize Theron and Seth Rogen

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker looks back at highlights of the 50th series of the quiz show so far, featuring team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell alongside guests including Ugo Monye, Jade Jones, Stuart Pearce, Osi Umenyiora and Sam Quek

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Glynn Purnell and Saliha Mahmood-Ahmed, plus special guest Jay Blades, and drinks expert Helen McGinn picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain takes a culinary road trip across Yorkshire, meeting fisherman Frank Powell near Bridlington and using some of his catch to cook baked sea trout with sun-dried tomatoes and lemons. She then heads to Huddersfield to sample local halloumi made by Syrian refugee Razan Alsous, creating watermelon and halloumi skewers. At home, Nadiya makes rhubarb and custard ice-cream sandwiches and Yorkshire puddings filled with strawberry jam and custard

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Chelsea v Leicester City in the FA Cup final

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.title: FA Cup Football
programme.desc: Classic moments involving this year's cup finalists, including Chelsea v Middlesbrough in 1997 and Leicester City v Manchester City in 1969

programme.title: Match of Their Day
programme.desc: Seven-time FA Cup-winner Ashley Cole joins Gary Lineker to look back at the best showpiece matches he played in for Arsenal and Chelsea

programme.title: FA Cup: The Road to Wembley
programme.desc: A look at the biggest stories of this season's FA Cup ahead of this weekend's final between Chelsea and Leicester City at Wembley Stadium

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Leicester City (Kick-off 5.15pm). Gary Lineker presents all the action from the showpiece match at Wembley Stadium. The Blues have won this competition on eight occasions, with six of those triumphs coming since the turn of the Millennium, most recently in 2018 when they overcame Manchester United 1-0 in the final. In contrast, the Foxes have never lifted the trophy and this is the first time they have played in the final since 1969. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Ian Wright

programme.title: I Can See Your Voice
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts the mystery singing game show, in which contestants aided by a celebrity panel must spot good singers without hearing them. Regular celebrity investigators Jimmy Carr, Alison Hammond and Amanda Holden are joined by guest Heather Small, as they assist Caitlin and Kristen from Warrington in picking out the best singers from a line-up that includes a singing wrestler, a model and a dentist

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as Chris Hollins, Dallas Campbell, Phil Daniels, Levi Roots, Lou Sanders, Jayde Adams, Lucy Porter and Dave Johns take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Rash is thrown into the deep end when Ethan signs him up for the finance meeting and his day gets even more complicated when an old friend arrives in the ED. Iain and Faith bond as they treat a dementia patient and her adoring husband, and Tina reminisces on her grief over Nate

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Action from today's Premiership fixtures, featuring Hibernian v Celtic at Easter Road, Rangers v Aberdeen at Ibrox and St Johnstone v Livingston at McDiarmid Park, as the teams concluded their league campaigns. Plus, the second leg of the play-off semi-final

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Southampton v Fulham at St Mary's Stadium and Brighton & Hove Albion v West Ham United at Amex Stadium. Fulham and Brighton are still battling it out to avoid relegation to the Championship, while the Hammers were looking to move a step closer to securing a place in Europe. Plus, Burnley v Leeds United and the Friday night contest between Newcastle United and Manchester City

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Leicester City. Gabby Logan presents action from the final at Wembley Stadium, as the sides battled it out for the prestigious trophy. The Blues secured their place in the showpiece match with a 1-0 last-four victory over Manchester City, while Leicester overcame Southampton by the same scoreline

programme.title: X+Y (2014)
programme.desc: A specially awkward teenager finds it difficult to communicate with people, but finds comfort in his passion for numbers. However, while training to take part in the forthcoming International Mathematical Olympiad, he finds love with a kindred spirit. Comedy drama, starring Asa Butterfield, Rafe Spall and Sally Hawkins

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gabby Logan presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Southampton v Fulham at St Mary's Stadium and Brighton & Hove Albion v West Ham United at Amex Stadium. Fulham and Brighton are still battling it out to avoid relegation to the Championship, while the Hammers were looking to move a step closer to securing a place in Europe. Plus, Burnley v Leeds United and the Friday night contest between Newcastle United and Manchester City

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Chelsea v Leicester City. Gabby Logan presents action from the final at Wembley Stadium, as the sides battled it out for the prestigious trophy. The Blues secured their place in the showpiece match with a 1-0 last-four victory over Manchester City, while Leicester overcame Southampton by the same scoreline

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: The Sunday Show
programme.desc: Martin Geissler presents the main issues from the world of politics and beyond, featuring interviews, discussion and analysis on the stories that matter to Scotland

programme.title: Sunday Morning Stories
programme.desc: Rev Kate Bottley hears from people who have given up their time to help others, including the UK's youngest chief executive and a couple who offered their home to a Syrian refugee

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: Rev Mandy Ralph of Annbank Parish Church, Ayr, and Fr David Wallace, Our Lady of Lourdes, Cardonald, lead todays Reflections on the theme of Unity. Music is by Celtic Worship

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich help a Bedfordshire woman pay tribute to her late husband. Maureen wants her new garden to be a reminder of her and her Graham's life together, with a sense of their old mountain views mixed with a Moroccan-cum-Cordoba style courtyard, inspired by their travels. Can Charlie and the Rich brothers transport this blank canvas to Spain on a £3,000 budget?

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Raj Bisram help two teams at Southwell racecourse in Nottinghamshire make a profit at auction. Hosted by Anita Manning

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones explores the Cathedral town of Exeter and, in Dementia Action Week, YolanDa Brown meets one local woman helping people with dementia to remain active

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers have their say on the BBC's output and there are behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple in the Welsh county of Ceredigion, looking to buy a country home with the potential for a spiritual retreat on a budget of £355,000

programme.title: Cars 3 (2017)
programme.desc: Constantly outshined and outraced by a fleet of hi-tech rookies, former champ Lightning McQueen must find a new way to win. Animated adventure, featuring the voices of Owen Wilson, Cristela Alonzo and Chris Cooper

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates footage of a lion pride on Kenya's Masai Mara that has reached a dangerous and unusual turning point where the future of the group hangs in the balance. The adult males that had defended the pride have all deserted the group, leaving two remaining adult females to protect their eight cubs from a wide range of threats, including rival prides, herds of buffalo and the constant menace of marauding hyenas. However, the greatest threat of all comes when the lions encounter humans

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith head to the Peak District to celebrate the 70th anniversary of the UK's first National Park. Matt joins a pioneering scheme to restore ruined farm buildings and meets 'legend' of the Peak District Gordon Miller, one of the area's early rangers and a last link with those who brought about the National Park. Charlotte channels her inner artist to help to create a giant anniversary art mural and records the special sounds of the Park's waterways, while Joe Crowley investigates new pollution laws causing controversy in the countryside

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The Roadshow is at Stonor Park in Oxfordshire, where finds include a rare group of Indian medals, Florence Nightingale's toolkit and the story of a remarkable female pilot from the Second World War. There is also an intriguing painting by an unknown artist, some computer generated art with a surprisingly high value and a collection of rare pearls from around the world

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne is concerned when a toddler displays troubling symptoms, while an overly protective mother struggles to cope with her daughter's teenage pregnancy and is insistent on keeping it low key, much to the frustration of new Pupil Midwife Nancy

programme.title: The Pursuit of Love
programme.desc: Fanny embraces motherhood and tries to live up to society's image of a good wife, while Linda throws herself into a lifestyle of all-night parties as her marriage to Tony almost immediately falls apart. When she is pulled away by an enigmatic stranger, she sees the chance to make a drastic change and start a whole new life

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including West Bromwich Albion v Liverpool at The Hawthorns. Sam Allardyce's men held the Reds to a 1-1 draw when the sides met at Anfield in December in what was the start of a downturn in form for Liverpool. Plus, Everton v Sheffield United, Crystal Palace v Aston Villa and Tottenham Hotspur v Wolverhampton Wanderers

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Action from the final Premiership fixtures of the season, featuring Hamilton Academical v Kilmarnock at Fountain of Youth Stadium, Motherwell v Ross County at Fir Park and St Mirren v Dundee United at Simple Digital Arena

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Julia Somerville, Angela Rippon and Gloria Hunniford report on the online electronics retailer that has left dozens of customers in the lurch. Plus, Julia hears from the learners in lockdown who found self-education more expensive than they'd expected, explaining how to avoid falling foul of the small print

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development progress of terraced houses in Carlisle and Abbey Wood in London, as well as a seven-year project to renovate a large period house in Torquay

programme.title: Fraud Squad
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley reports on the dedicated fraud-fighters pursuing the criminals who steal £40billion a year from the public purse. This edition features the amateur actress who put on a performance to evade justice for a showstopping £750,000 fraud and the school business manager who embezzled over £200,000

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: How a mountain rescue team fight its way through appalling conditions to rescue a walker on Snowdon. Plus, the mountain biker who crash-landed after a jump, leading his friends to believe he's broken his neck. Presented by Nick Knowles

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Jonathan Pratt are in Nottinghamshire to help two teams look for items of value at the Newark Antiques and Collectable fair. Presenter Charlie Ross hits the fair to see if anyone can help him with a mystery object and later in the show the team heads to Derby for the highs and lows of the auction

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Billie, Seb, Victoria and Joshua vie to find the single 'unbeatable' answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges artists to make something amazing for the homes of people they've never met. In Manchester, the buyers want to celebrate the day their lives changed for ever -10 years ago when they won the National Lottery. Will it be a molten glass specialist, an Icelandic abstract artist or a self-taught metal sculptor who fulfils their brief?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman visits Norfolk to help a father and daughter who hope their £700,000 budget will provide them with a large equestrian property and annexe to house their family of four, along with their horse. Nicki also heads to the Norfolk Broads and learns about the restoration efforts of the Norfolk Wherry Trust before taking to the water to sail one of these traditional wooden boats

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore is at Witley Recycling Centre, salvaging three unwanted gems so they can shine again. A broken display cabinet finds its way to furniture artist Chloe Kempster, designer Daniel Heath needs to set a 1970s dressing table back on the right path, and Sarah tries to help a chandelier gleam again

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: For their last outing, Arusha Irvine and David Harper enjoy a whistle-stop tour of the Lake District, battling each other - and the elements - in a sporty classic car. David's hunt for antiques unearths a secret map issued by the RAF, but with time ticking before the final showdown Roo gambles on a Georgian long case clock. The duo then head to North Shields for the auction

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Sue Barker looks back at highlights of the 50th series of the quiz show so far, featuring team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell alongside guests including Ugo Monye, Jade Jones, Stuart Pearce, Osi Umenyiora and Sam Quek

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Karen snaps when offered a free meal from the cafe, sick of everyone judging her and her family. Nancy reluctantly agrees to babysit for Sharon, but while looking after Albie she feels unwell and only just manages to put the child back in his playpen before collapsing to the ground with an epileptic seizure

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Actors and comedians Liz Carr and Miles Jupp, actor and writer Samson Kayo and broadcaster Anneka Rice.join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts the satirical quiz, with panellists Lemn Sissay and Jo Brand joining regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's headlines

programme.title: Pilgrimage: The Road to Santiago
programme.desc: The seven celebrities continue their trek along the Camino de Santiago pilgrimage to their destination of the Cathedral of Santiago de Compostela. The Rev Kate Bottley discovers a fountain of free-flowing wine, while Ed Byrne meets a man who believes money is the root of all evil. As the group bonds strengthen, JJ Chalmers and Neil Morrissey open up about traumatic experiences that have shaped their lives, while Debbie McGee leaves a stone bearing the name of her late husband, Paul Daniels, at the foot of the cathedral's famous Iron Cross. Narrated by Lee Ingleby

programme.title: Football, Racism and Social Media
programme.desc: For four days, the footballing world, with support from across sport, used its voice - by staying silent on social media. The boycott was the latest attempt at tackling the racist abuse suffered by black and Asian players. Yet even those at the centre of the campaign conceded little would change as a result. Nesta McGregor speaks to victims, social media companies and those charged with cleaning up the sport. Why is the abuse happening, what can be done to stop it and what are the consequences?

programme.title: Van Der Valk
programme.desc: Marc Warren steps into the shoes of Barry Foster to take on the role of Dutch detective Van der Valk in this updated adaptation of the Amsterdam-based crime drama. Two seemingly unrelated men are found murdered in different parts of the city on the same day. Are the deaths connected, and if so, could there be a link to the disappearance of a student activist? Commissaris Van der Valk must uncover the truth while also dealing with an over-eager rookie cop joining his tight-knit team. Maimie McCoy, Luke Allen-Gale and Elliot Barnes Worrell co-star

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: The BRIT Awards 2021
programme.desc: The comedian and television presenter Jack Whitehall hosts the annual star-studded award ceremony, live from London's O2, in front of an audience of the nation's key workers, which will be the first major music event to welcome back a live audience in over a year, with 4,000 people in attendance. programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Tv presenter Scarlett Moffatt and Radio 1's Jordan North join hosts Martin and Roman Kemp on the entertainment show, while resident chef Shivi Ramoutar helps the father and son develop their culinary skills

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by Loose Women's Brenda Edwards and there are recipes for honey cake, and gurnard and chicken pie, and there is a food adventure to Arles. Guest chefs Mark Birchall and John Hooker also drop by with delicious creations of their own

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner return with the culinary show, beginning with a host of dishes that can work for breakfast, lunch or even dinner. Lisa prepares bread and butter pudding made with pain au chocolat, while John rustles up a rhubarb cocktail and gives an omelette masterclass

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef heads to St Tropez, where he explores the seafood market in search of ingredients to cook aboard a yacht. James also drops in on old friend Michel Roux and they prepare a rather special barbecue using ingredients from his garden

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage from Newbury, including the 3.00 London Gold Cup and 3.35 Lockinge Stakes. Plus, three races from Newmarket. With analysis from Jason Weaver, reports by Rishi Persad, Matt Chapman and Luke Harvey, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. A teenager rides her hoverboard into a swimming pool, a man gets his beard caught in a power tool and a drone crashes into an unfortunate child's face

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Terry, Alex, Oyin and Julie pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Terry, Alex, Oyin and Julie work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Barry in the Vale of Glamorgan for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow
programme.desc: The comedian hosts an all-star version of Name That Tune, with Shane Richie, Gabby Logan, Vick Hope and Richard Blackwood trying to identify songs played by a house band in the shortest time possible and have a chance at winning the £25,000 jackpot. Singer Sophie Ellis-Bextor makes a guest appearance at the piano

programme.title: Game of Talents
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the game show, with two contestants pairing up with Nick Grimshaw and Clara Amfo to try to win big money by uncovering the secret talents of a range of mystery performers

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host welcomes singers Tom Jones and Paloma Faith, Guardians of the Galaxy, Spectre and Blade Runner 2049 star Dave Bautista, comedian James Acaster and former boxer Nicola Adams. Plus, rock duo Royal Blood perform in the studio

programme.title: Con Air (1997)
programme.desc: Honourably discharged Army Ranger sergeant Cameron Poe hitches a ride home on a maximum-security flight conveying some of America's deadliest criminals. When they hijack the plane in mid-flight, led by criminal mastermind Cyrus the Virus, Poe is forced to intervene. Meanwhile, US marshal Vince Larkin has an ongoing battle on the ground to prevent the plane from being blown to pieces. Director Simon West's blockbusting action thriller, starring Nicolas Cage, John Cusack, John Malkovich, Steve Buscemi, Ving Rhames and Colm Meaney

programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Dare Master

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Fluffy the cat has found the robotic vacuum cleaner and is riding it about the house like a daredevil, leaving an opening for the raccoons to strike.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mercury eats some rancid cheese from the rubbish, granting the power of rancid breath so the raccoons can go on a robbing spree.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: While visiting Lake Erie a relaxing day at the beach gets all washed up when Ben Gwen and Grandpa Max come face-to-face with Hex and his summer intern Kevin!

programme.desc: Four ninja warriors battle dark Lord Garmadon and his skeleton army

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Four ninja warriors battle dark Lord Garmadon and his skeleton army

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Weekend Best!
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas, Aljaz Skorjanec and Joel Corry join Martin and Roman Kemp for more celebrity chat, music and comedy, as well as games and light-hearted challenges in the which the father-and-son duo go up against their guests

programme.title: Australia with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia continues her 12,000km journey across the continent with a tour of the Northern Territory. She embarks on a crocodile safari in the wetlands of the Northern Territory, before relaxing in Darwin, home of the Ghan train. She then hits the open road with a retiree who now travels the country - or as the Aussies call them 'grey nomads' - to discover life on one of Australia's vast cattle stations

programme.title: Alan Titchmarsh: Spring into Summer
programme.desc: Alan celebrates the great outdoors, offering tips on gardening and farming and getting celebrity guests involved in planting schemes and food tasting. Call the Midwife star Cliff Parisi tells Alan about his role on the show, Camilla Bassett Smith is back in her cottage garden and Jo Behari transforms an old garden bench

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner return with the culinary show, beginning with a host of dishes that can work for breakfast, lunch or even dinner. Lisa prepares bread and butter pudding made with pain au chocolat, while John rustles up a rhubarb cocktail and gives an omelette masterclass

programme.title: Game of Talents
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the game show, with two contestants pairing up with Nick Grimshaw and Clara Amfo to try to win big money by uncovering the secret talents of a range of mystery performers

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Barry in the Vale of Glamorgan for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Australian Ninja Warrior
programme.desc: Rebecca Maddern, Ben Fordham and Freddie Flintoff present the Down Under version of the show in which contestants take on a formidable assault course in Melbourne, with obstacles including the Floating Steps, the Swing Surfer, the Tuning Forks, Rolling Steel, Spinball Wizard and the Double Warped Walls

programme.title: Peter Pan (2003)
programme.desc: The adventures of the boy who never grows old, as he whisks Wendy Darling and her brothers away to the magical world of Neverland - where they come face to face with fearsome Captain Hook. Adaptation of JM Barrie's fantasy novel, starring Jeremy Sumpter, Rachel Hurd-Wood, Jason Isaacs and Lynn Redgrave

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Ruth Davidson, Mark King, Ferne McCann and Dan Walker pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard presents a celebrity edition of the quiz, with the Rev Richard Coles, Toyah Willcox and Sharron Davies answering questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them (2016)
programme.desc: British wizard Newt Scamander arrives in 1920s New York City with a bagful of magical creatures as part of a global study of fantastic beasts, but gets caught up in a conflict when some of the creatures in his care are accidentally released after an inadvertent switching of suitcases with a baker. With a strange, dark force terrorising the Big Apple, Newt needs to find some allies to help him round up the beasts, while saving his own skin in the meantime. Harry Potter prequel written for the screen by JK Rowling, starring Eddie Redmayne, with Katherine Waterston, Colin Farrell, Samantha Morton, Jon Voight, Dan Fogler and Carmen Ejogo

programme.title: The Queen and Her Cousins with Alexander Armstrong
programme.desc: As the Queen turns 95, Alexander Armstrong embarks on a royal road trip of a lifetime. He travels across the country to meet Elizabeth II's cousins, who share what it means to be part of this extraordinary family. Using the monarch's vast family tree as his road map, Alexander meets relatives from many branches of her family - from the ancient lineage of the Mountbatten dynasty, to the Queen Mother's Bowes-Lyon side - and an international princess

programme.title: Trevor McDonald's Indian Train Adventure
programme.desc: Part two of two. The newsreader continues his eight-day journey across India aboard luxury train the Maharajas Express, which recreates the heritage of India's richest kings. On the second leg of the trip, Trevor discovers the reasons why India is rapidly overtaking Britain as an economic superpower. He also visits an unusual temple where people worship rats, finds out how the Taj Mahal is being damaged by the effects of pollution, and concludes his journey at the site of Mahatma Gandhi's assassination

programme.title: Trevor McDonald & Charlene White: Has George Floyd Changed Britain?
programme.desc: Approaching the one-year anniversary of the death of George Floyd, Trevor McDonald and Charlene White explore the impact for people living in the UK. This documentary investigates the consequences of the tragedy, and the conversations that have followed in Britain

programme.desc: Johnny Vegas, Aljaz Skorjanec and Joel Corry join Martin and Roman Kemp for more celebrity chat, music and comedy, as well as games and light-hearted challenges in the which the father-and-son duo go up against their guests

programme.title: Australia with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia continues her 12,000km journey across the continent with a tour of the Northern Territory. She embarks on a crocodile safari in the wetlands of the Northern Territory, before relaxing in Darwin, home of the Ghan train. She then hits the open road with a retiree who now travels the country - or as the Aussies call them 'grey nomads' - to discover life on one of Australia's vast cattle stations

programme.title: Alan Titchmarsh: Spring into Summer
programme.desc: Alan celebrates the great outdoors, offering tips on gardening and farming and getting celebrity guests involved in planting schemes and food tasting. Call the Midwife star Cliff Parisi tells Alan about his role on the show, Camilla Bassett Smith is back in her cottage garden and Jo Behari transforms an old garden bench

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner return with the culinary show, beginning with a host of dishes that can work for breakfast, lunch or even dinner. Lisa prepares bread and butter pudding made with pain au chocolat, while John rustles up a rhubarb cocktail and gives an omelette masterclass

programme.title: Game of Talents
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the game show, with two contestants pairing up with Nick Grimshaw and Clara Amfo to try to win big money by uncovering the secret talents of a range of mystery performers

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern heads to Barry in the Vale of Glamorgan for the game show in which he travels around the UK setting members of the public unusual challenges, giving them the chance to win anything from the price of their petrol to £1,000 in the final game.

programme.title: Australian Ninja Warrior
programme.desc: Rebecca Maddern, Ben Fordham and Freddie Flintoff present the Down Under version of the show in which contestants take on a formidable assault course in Melbourne, with obstacles including the Floating Steps, the Swing Surfer, the Tuning Forks, Rolling Steel, Spinball Wizard and the Double Warped Walls

programme.title: Peter Pan (2003)
programme.desc: The adventures of the boy who never grows old, as he whisks Wendy Darling and her brothers away to the magical world of Neverland - where they come face to face with fearsome Captain Hook. Adaptation of JM Barrie's fantasy novel, starring Jeremy Sumpter, Rachel Hurd-Wood, Jason Isaacs and Lynn Redgrave

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Ruth Davidson, Mark King, Ferne McCann and Dan Walker pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard presents a celebrity edition of the quiz, with the Rev Richard Coles, Toyah Willcox and Sharron Davies answering questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Fantastic Beasts and Where to Find Them (2016)
programme.desc: British wizard Newt Scamander arrives in 1920s New York City with a bagful of magical creatures as part of a global study of fantastic beasts, but gets caught up in a conflict when some of the creatures in his care are accidentally released after an inadvertent switching of suitcases with a baker. With a strange, dark force terrorising the Big Apple, Newt needs to find some allies to help him round up the beasts, while saving his own skin in the meantime. Harry Potter prequel written for the screen by JK Rowling, starring Eddie Redmayne, with Katherine Waterston, Colin Farrell, Samantha Morton, Jon Voight, Dan Fogler and Carmen Ejogo

programme.title: The Queen and Her Cousins with Alexander Armstrong
programme.desc: As the Queen turns 95, Alexander Armstrong embarks on a royal road trip of a lifetime. He travels across the country to meet Elizabeth II's cousins, who share what it means to be part of this extraordinary family. Using the monarch's vast family tree as his road map, Alexander meets relatives from many branches of her family - from the ancient lineage of the Mountbatten dynasty, to the Queen Mother's Bowes-Lyon side - and an international princess

programme.title: Trevor McDonald's Indian Train Adventure
programme.desc: Part two of two. The newsreader continues his eight-day journey across India aboard luxury train the Maharajas Express, which recreates the heritage of India's richest kings. On the second leg of the trip, Trevor discovers the reasons why India is rapidly overtaking Britain as an economic superpower. He also visits an unusual temple where people worship rats, finds out how the Taj Mahal is being damaged by the effects of pollution, and concludes his journey at the site of Mahatma Gandhi's assassination

programme.title: Trevor McDonald & Charlene White: Has George Floyd Changed Britain?
programme.desc: Approaching the one-year anniversary of the death of George Floyd, Trevor McDonald and Charlene White explore the impact for people living in the UK. This documentary investigates the consequences of the tragedy, and the conversations that have followed in Britain

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of a trainee forensic psychologist, whose body has been dumped in a Northumberland landfill site, surroundings that make evidence of how she died impossible to verify. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A climber plunges to his death from a cliff face and a police informant claims it was no accident. A re-enactment of the incident adds fuel to the fire, and Wycliffe braces himself to interview the dead man's best friend. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters, Lynn Farleigh, Christian Solimeno and Paul Venables

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Bert struggles to come up with the money to repay loan sharks Alastair and Norman Tonken, who gave him £1,000 to clear his debts. Eleanor falls ill and Martin diagnoses a strangulated hernia, but when the ambulance goes to the wrong village, he has no alternative but to perform the emergency operation himself in his surgery. With Martin Clunes, Ian McNeice and Louise Jameson

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Mrs Tishell decides she can no longer hide her love for Martin and, fuelled by a cocktail of drugs, takes drastic action. While James Henry is in her care, she boards a bus out of Portwenn and leaves a message for Dr Ellingham to 'meet them at the castle', forcing Martin, Louisa, Ruth and PC Penhale to mount a desperate search for the pair. Starring Martin Clunes, Selina Cadell and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The hunt for the killer of a seemingly innocent family man brings Ronnie into contact with his old boss, former DI Natalie Chandler, whose father is accused of the crime. She wastes no time in persuading the detective to go above and beyond the call of duty to exonerate her dad and find the real culprit - even if that means getting him into trouble with an increasingly frustrated Wes. Drama, starring Bradley Walsh and Ben Bailey Smith, with Harriet Walter, Martin Jarvis and Roy Hudd

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When the body of an elderly woman is discovered at a well-known suicide spot, Ronnie soon detects foul play, with suspicion falling on the victim's son and daughter-in-law, as well as a doctor who has recently arrived in the UK. However, even after a confession is secured, Joe remains baffled as to the motive, and Kate struggles to remain professional - jeopardising her career and leaving Jake to prosecute the case alone. Guest starring Haydn Gwynne

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Dour DI Jack Frost suspects a man he arrested years before for a series of bizarre assaults on children could have returned to the area and recommenced his unsavoury activities. He tracks down the culprit, who swears he has turned over a new leaf, and decides not to charge him - a decision that shocks fellow sleuth DS Maud, whose misgivings are later justified in tragic fashion. Starring David Jason, Peter Gunn, Susannah Doyle, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Gwyneth Powell

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth finds himself in interesting company as English socialites and American millionaires rub shoulders on board a train bound for the French Riviera. Passengers include oil magnate Rufus Van Aldin (Elliott Gould) and his daughter Ruth (Jaime Murray) - to whom he has given a magnificent ruby known as the Heart of Fire. With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A music teacher asks Holmes to discover the identity of a mysterious bearded cyclist who often follows at a distance as she makes her way to work. Period crime drama, guest starring Barbara Wilshere, Michael Siberry and John Castle

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Dempsey is accused of murdering a small-time criminal, forcing Makepeace to put her career on the line as she attempts to uncover evidence to support his claims of innocence. Detective drama, starring Michael Brandon and Glynis Barber

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: The resources of CI5 are stretched when a mentally unbalanced former soldier starts killing hospital staff, believing them responsible for the death of his wife and for the brain damage suffered by his young daughter. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is held by the police after a car he is importing from Europe is impounded by drugs officers, so Terry goes in search of those he suspects are responsible for his boss' predicament - the vehicle's original owners. Light-hearted crime drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Champions
programme.desc: The trio pose as domestic staff to protect the life of a former dictator. Inevitably, domestic duties give way to dangerous circumstances. Fantasy drama, starring William Gaunt, Alexandra Bastedo and Stuart Damon

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur persuades a reluctant Terry to make a comeback in the boxing ring to take on Jackie Wilson, the opponent he faced prior to losing his licence. However, Wilson's wife soon appears claiming her husband is unable to fight. Vintage comedy drama, starring George Cole and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Big Fish Off
programme.desc: Former footballer Wayne Bridge and ex-rugby union star Kyran Bracken join team captains Ali Hamidi and Dean Macey for the angling challenge

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Chat-show host Jonathan Ross, comedienne and writer Katy Brand, Watchdog's Chris Hollins and model Jo Wood compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by taking on one of the show's resident trivia experts in a test of their general knowledge. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Bradley Walsh asks the questions

programme.title: Octopussy (1983)
programme.desc: James Bond investigates the mysterious death of a British agent in East Berlin, a mission that leads him to a glamorous, international jewel thief. He discovers one of her criminal cohorts, an Indian gangster, is in league with a crazed Soviet general who plans to heat up the Cold War by detonating a nuclear bomb in an American Air Force base. Spy adventure, with Roger Moore, Maud Adams, Steven Berkoff and Louis Jourdan

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie's former girlfriend visits from East Germany and he finds himself framed for carrying out an assassination attempt - leaving Doyle with no choice but to bring him in for questioning. Drama, starring Lewis Collins and Martin Shaw

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle investigate the death of a burglar who was killed after seeing classified information about a foreign country, and resort to theft in a bid to uncover the embassy's sinister secrets. Liz Fraser and Michael Elphick guest star

programme.title: Made in Britain

programme.title: MotoGP Live
programme.desc: The Grand Prix de France. All the action from the Moto3, Moto2 and MotoGP races in the fifth round of the season at Le Mans. Jack Miller won the MotoGP race last time out at the Spanish Grand Prix in Jerez after Yamaha's Fabio Quartararo suffered an arm-pump issue while leading the race and went on to finish in 13th position. Miller's Ducati team mate, Francecso Bagnaia, claimed second place and that was enough for him to overtake Quartararo at the top of the championship standings. With commentary from Steve Day and Matt Birt and pit-lane reports by Simon Crafar

programme.desc: A look back at motorcycling in the late 1980s and early 1990s, which is widely regarded as one of the most exciting eras in the history of two-wheel racing and featured the likes of Wayne Rainey, Eddie Lawson, Freddie Spencer, Wayne Gardner, Mick Doohan and Ron Haslam. Including action from memorable races that involved these riders competing against each other

programme.title: Rambo: First Blood Part II (1985)
programme.desc: Incarcerated Special Forces veteran John Rambo is offered the chance for freedom by former commander Colonel Trautman if he takes up the offer to return to the jungles of Vietnam on a life-or-death mission to rescue American PoWs. During the course of the operation, he develops strong feelings for his guide, a female freedom fighter - but tragedy strikes, leaving him more determined than ever to exact a bloody and explosive revenge. Action adventure sequel, starring Sylvester Stallone, Richard Crenna, Julia Nickson-Soul, Steven Berkoff, George Kee Cheung, Andy Wood and Martin Kove

programme.title: Rambo: First Blood Part II (1985)
programme.desc: Incarcerated Special Forces veteran John Rambo is offered the chance for freedom by former commander Colonel Trautman if he takes up the offer to return to the jungles of Vietnam on a life-or-death mission to rescue American PoWs. programme.title: Jeremy Wade's Mighty Rivers
programme.desc: Biologist, extreme angler and naturalist Jeremy Wade heads to the Amazon. Together with its tributaries, it contains an incredible 20 per cent of Earth's fresh water, and the immense forests it sustains generate a quarter of the oxygen needed for life on the planet. Disgorging more water than the next nine biggest rivers combined, this waterway is indeed a mighty affair. By tracing the epic migration of the dorado catfish, Jeremy discovers the issues putting this great river at risk - from leaking pipelines to giant dams. However, he also discovers tales of surprising hope

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
