programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his trusty 1913 Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide, Michael Portillo ventures east through Romania to the shores of the Black Sea. Along the way he encounters a bloodsucking vampire in Transylvania and brown bears in the Carpathian forest, before visiting a fairytale castle with modern conveniences in Sinaia, striking oil in Ploiesti, tapping into the nation's musical soul in Bucharest and loading cargo from a crane in Constanta. This leg of his journey ends at Constanta, where Michael explores what is now emerging as Europe's largest grain port

programme.desc: Derek collects a Jaguar Mark 2 and explains why they made such poor getaway cars for 1960s bank robbers. A customer considers a Morris Marina as a Christmas present for his wife

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The team is under pressure with a record number of cars to auction, but Derek finds room for one more - a replica AC Cobra. A couple are reunited with their Volvo breakdown truck

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A classic car fan with a love of the Jaguar E-Type reluctantly sends his dream car to auction, hoping to buy a motorhome with the money. Two Ford Escorts get Dave's pulse racing

programme.desc: A widow reluctantly parts with her husband's treasured collection of Rolls-Royces and Bentleys, and an impulse buyer finds one of the cars a new home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: At Trinity College, Dublin, Michael Portillo discovers one of Ireland's greatest treasures, and also explores the house and gardens of Powerscourt in County Wicklow

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey across Ireland, travelling from Navan to Mullingar. Along the way, he gets up to speed with modern archaeology in County Meath, where he uncovers a controversial Victorian dig at the sacred Hill of Tara. Later, he investigates leaping salmon in Leixslip, and in Mullingar, bangs the drum for the town's traditional marching band

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his Irish journey from Dromod to Sligo. Along the way he discovers how the landscape inspired the poet WB Yeats and learns to make a potato pancake

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the last leg of Michael Portillo's long journey from Wexford to Westport, a fashionable Victorian seaweed bath is followed by a steamy scene in Ballina. On tenterhooks in Foxford, Michael discovers the visionary charity of an entrepreneurial nun, learns of a double tragedy at Clew Bay and begins a pilgrimage to the summit of Croagh Patrick, Ireland's holy mountain

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on train journeys through Edwardian Britain, exploring the grand estates of Norfolk and a bivouac on Brownsea Island on the way. He also learns the ropes aboard an Edwardian wherry on the Norfolk Broads

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores an Edwardian utopia in Letchworth, where he meets a fourth generation citizen who introduces him to the town's community spirit. He then heads towards London, and discovers a favoured haunt of King Edward VII, before sampling the monarch's favourite tipple, the King's Ginger

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Croydon, where he hears the story of a mixed-race composer who was once celebrated but is now forgotten. At the Three Bridges station, he is delighted to find a recently built depot and its fleet of new Class 700 trains - and is given the great honour of washing one down. In Lewes, he makes a beeline for Charleston, the home of artists Vanessa Bell and Duncan Grant, and in Shoreham-by-Sea, Michael discovers how magnificent Edwardians in flying machines took off from the oldest licensed airport in the country

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to Chichester, West Sussex, where he encounters an Edwardian motoring duo patronised by King Edward VII. After a grand tour of their factory, he is treated to some rest and relaxation in one of their finest vehicles. Arriving in the resort of Southsea, Hampshire, Michael heads for the King's Theatre, where he treads the boards in a community production of Lads in the Village. Crossing the Solent, the presenter boards the Island Line to travel along the pier in a 1930s London Underground carriage, then hops on to the Isle of Wight heritage line bound for Wootton

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: The Meldrew household is disrupted by a pesky plumber whose penchant for morbid practical jokes would try the patience of a saint. David Renwick's award-winning comedy, starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor and Margaret are trapped in the shed by a swarm of escaped bees - and not unexpectedly where the mishap-prone Meldrews are concerned, that is just the start of their troubles. Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie star

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor takes up life drawing classes and Margaret is only too happy to see the back of him - until she learns the subject is a nude woman whom he has volunteered to drive home. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: The Rolling Stones at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of the band's performances from the BBC archives, in a programme originally broadcast in 2012 to celebrate their 50th anniversary. Songs include The Last Time, Let's Spend The Night Together, Get Off of My Cloud, Honky Tonk Women, Gimme Shelter, Miss You and Start Me Up. Plus, footage of the band performing 19th Nervous Breakdown on Top of the Pops in 1966, which was used in a 1960s BBC documentary about women with depression

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties

After a first course that whistles and wows, palates need to be cleansed for the main course, but a chilly chat about ice cream leaves Louise red in the face. On the final night, Louise and Danny confound everyone's expectations with a sailing-based starter. A spot of model behaviour excites the guests, and a mountain of meringue impresses, but is it enough to win the £1,000 prize?

programme.desc: The second visit is to Elmfield B&B in Devon, where retired politician Martin Perry and housekeeper Tracey Barrett pride themselves on keeping things traditional

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at the Chocolate Box in Bournemouth, where the hosts playful approach leaves their guests questioning their business credentials

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to the Buckle Seaford B&B in East Sussex, where an afternoon of football confirms the women's suspicions about hosts Julie and Jason Martin's competitive streak

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time, and Causilgey Manor owners Robin and Brenda want to get to grips with Julie and Jason's gripes over privacy

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie prepares to end the year in style by throwing a Christmas dinner to remember. However, with the ancient range cooker out of action and the 19th-century bread oven put into service cooking the turkey, it's a tall order to deliver a five-course extravaganza for 17 guests. Angel sets Dick the task of finding and chopping the perfect logs for her Christmas table display in their 12-acre grounds. Christmas cheer threatens to run dry for south Londoners Lee and Belinda as freezing temperatures and a broken boiler put their first ever festive event in peril

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: A recent storm leaves Lee and Belinda's castle keep in need of urgent repairs - and Lee must face his fear of heights if he is to repair the hole in the roof

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: With the holiday season fast approaching, Stephanie enlists close family friend Gerry and her mother Isobel to get her B&B room finished in time. Lee and Belinda plan to create a new outdoor events space for their first wedding booking. To go the extra mile, the south London couple aim to construct a water feature, and call on Dick for advice - but things don't go to plan. Jayne and Steve rope in their friends to finish renovating the ground floor, and the promise of a thank-you meal keeps spirits high, although dinner could end up being served cold

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: With their children having left home, Maureen and Brian Kellengher want to downsize and enjoy their retirement. However, while they love the idea of staying close to family and friends along the Suffolk-Essex border, they're excited about starting a whole new life in Lanzarote. Jonnie Irwin shows them three beautiful properties for their budget of £320,000 to tempt them to stay in the UK and Jasmine Harman goes all out with three properties for £225,000 in Lanzarote

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Tim and Lili Maher have sold their house in north London and with retirement on the horizon they want to embrace a completely new way of life and plan to spend most of the year on the Northern Costa Blanca, but also want to return to the UK to enjoy the English summers. So the pressures on to find not one but two new homes both home and away. Ben Hillman flies the flag for the UK by showing three properties in Great Yarmouth for their UK budget of £120,000 while Jasmine Harman goes all out on Spanish charm in Northern Costa Blanca with their away budget of £240,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps Kathryn and Mark find a home on the Kassandra Peninsula in the north of Greece, with plenty of room for the family and near to the beach on a budget of £80,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: John and Alison Peart from Devon are at a crossroads. With Alison hoping to retire in the next year and John being made redundant, they're focusing all their attention on finding a new home. While Alison wants to split her time to spend half the year in Dorset, close to their family, and the other six months in Tenerife, her favourite place, John would happily relocate permanently to Tenerife. Laura Hamilton shows them three fantastic homes in Dorset for their maximum £400,000 UK budget while Jonnie Irwin tempts them with three amazing properties in Tenerife for up to £235,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Steve and Aniko want to escape the rat race and move to the peace and quiet of the French countryside in west Brittany. Steve is a music composer and Aniko a singer, so their dream would be to be able to create their own recording studio at home. With a budget of £90,000, property expert Jonnie Irwin lines up five options that he hopes could be just the ticket for the pair

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer help two couples searching in south London. Elliot and James want to buy their first place together but are struggling to pin down exactly where. Their favourite areas are Putney and Richmond, but their money does not go as far as they would like. Pam and Ross are in Wimbledon, where former tennis professional Ross works. However, having been stung after the 2009 housing crisis, they are understandably cautious

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer return to find out how some of the families they have helped are getting on since they decided whether to redesign their problematic homes or sell up and move on. They begin with Paul and Dawn from the first edition back in April 2015, who were at loggerheads over what to do with their detached country home. Its isolated location on the outskirts of Huddersfield made it perfect for astronomy fanatic Paul, but Dawn was desperate to move, hating its dysfunctional layout

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jon Richardson and Lucy Beaumont take on Roisin Conaty and Joe Wilkinson in the comedy words-and-numbers game, while Bec Hill joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Paramedics: Britain's Lifesavers
programme.desc: As the UK enters its fourth week of lockdown, West Midlands Ambulance Service comes to the aid of the staff of care homes, who are feeling forgotten as the virus claims the lives of so many residents. It falls on crews like Julie and Adam to provide help and reassurance. Back in the control centre, Irum has had fast-track training and is plunged into the stark realities of her new role, while a national minute's silence for NHS and care workers holds a particular significance for her

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on shows including SAS: Who Dares Wins, Dragons' Den, Three Families, Headspace: Guide to Sleep, Secret Crush, Lego Masters USA and This Morning. They also proffer their thoights on a BBC News report about taking holidays abroad as the pandemic rules on travel are relaxed

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows, be they entertainment juggernauts, hard-hitting documentaries, news reports or gritty dramas

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson welcomes 24-year-old Sian from Norfolk, a petrol head who wants to find a man she can love more than her beloved car, and 33-year-old fashion entrepreneur Tansel from London. He is looking to settle down and hopes that losing ignoring the clothes for once will help him find the right girl

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Countdown's Nick Hewer joins singer and TV personality Stacey Solomon, the Kaiser Chiefs' Ricky Wilson and Diversity dancer Perri Kiely for the baking challenge. Presenters Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding guide them through the assorted tasks, and Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith pass judgment on their skills. On the menu are scones, a devilish technical, and a showstopper challenge in the form of self-portrait croquembouches. Which of the famous faces will impress the judges?

programme.title: Beat the Chef
programme.desc: Today's contestant prepares goat curry for the first time on the series, pitting her recipe against Michelin-starred Mark Sargeant's best efforts. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: Coach Trip
programme.desc: The tourists arrive in Bilbao, where Brendan leads the group to a sheep farm, before splashing the cash on a boat party

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at the Bowling Green in Chorlton-cum-Hardy, Manchester, where owner Mark Canny hopes his newly refurbished rooms and head for hosting will secure him success

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Redhill Holidays in Whitbourne near Worcester, a glamping site with pole-perched premier pods - and an unwelcome population of insects

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week comes from the Mode Lytham Hotel in Lytham, Lancashire, where husband and wife Andrew and Qidi Whitaker hope their hotel will bag them best in show

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Villa Farm Cottage in Thornton Curtis in Lincolnshire, where perfectionist Petra Coates and her partner Martin Littlefield run a tight ship. When the guests arrive, the bathroom tiles take Andrew back to his public swimming pool days and Mark has a moan about a missing en suite. In the morning, the guests are spoilt for choice as Petra provides a German cold meat feast followed by an English breakfast cooked by Martin. However, Andrew and Qidi's feedback about the tiling leaves Martin and Petra planning to hit back on payment day

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and settle some scores. Mark from the Bowling Green Hotel questions Martin and Petra about a lack of chairs and then gives Qidi and Andrew's noisy fridge comments a frosty reception

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie gets green-fingered as she sets out to restore the chateau's original greenhouse, which has recently been damaged in a storm. Lee and Belinda continue to expand their new outdoor venue. When their first wedding falls through, they fortunately get a new booking for a 60th birthday, but then end up in a race against time to get the garden party ready. With 12 acres of land to enjoy, Dick and Angel make sure their Wellington boots have a home, creating bootjacks for the newly renovated boot room

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Stephanie has a month to convert her old stable into a studio for the launch of her art weekends, but when she doesn't see eye to eye with mother Isobel about the design, renovations could take longer than anticipated. Belinda's promised a heated outdoor swimming pool on the chateau's website, so Lee starts constructing a solar panel system. Dick saves cash by getting creative on a new lighting project for Angel's treasury

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Boston to help change the fortunes of a failing Italian restaurant run by two brothers. The eatery's decline began when one of the pair started embezzling money to fund his prescription drug addiction, and things only got worse when the he was sentenced to four years in prison. With the time now served, he has returned, but must first regain his brother's trust before he can save their business

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Bridgeport, Connecticut, to visit Cafe Tavolini, which is being neglected by husband and wife managers teetering on the edge of divorce. The estranged couple's two children are struggling to save the business from their parents' disputes, and the visiting chef tries to shock the pair into stopping the restaurant from becoming a casualty of their quarrel

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Shane and Sharon dream of owning a holiday home on the Costa del Sol that they can escape to during the winter to enjoy the area's all-year round outdoor lifestyle. With a budget of £135,000, they want an apartment or town house close to the beach around La Cala de Mijas, with two bedrooms so their family can come to visit. Danni Menzies rises to the challenge to find them the perfect property

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Grandparents Alan and Elisa Bowers have lived in Plymouth for all of their lives and are itching for a new start, but are torn between moving to the beautiful Cornwall coast to stay close to family, or relocating to Lanzarote, their dream location. Laura Hamilton shows them three properties in Cornwall for their £300,000 UK budget while Scarlette Douglas has some amazing places in Lanzarote for their budget of £270,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retired couple Dabi and Devi can't wait to wave goodbye to the grey skies of Croydon, south London, and head to the sunshine and heat in the seaside town of Gandia in Valencia. They're looking for a second home where they can spend up to six months at a time. With a budget of £180,000, Lee Juggurnauth lines up five impressive options for them to consider

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Retired bus driver Sue and postal worker Ali are relocating from Weston-super-Mare to inland Spain and hope Ben Hillman can find them a permanent home on their budget of £70,000

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect travels to Macclesfield to meet a first-time builder who hopes to transform an allotment shed into a Doctor Who-esque, multi-functional play den for less than £300. He also visits a spectacular live-in tree house in Scotland, and creates a medieval-style lock for the wood cabin, with Will's help. The Italian road trip takes in a holiday lodge made almost entirely from glass in the Dolomites

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Andy Stakes and Jeanette Hardy, who are building a new house on top of a sheer cliff on the west coast of Scotland. The pair want to spend £250,000 on a two-bedroom, glass-fronted and earth-sheltered home for themselves and their two beloved Newfoundland dogs. However, construction on such an exposed site is fraught with danger and the pair face no end of problems throughout the build, and a last minute design change that begs the biggest question: even if Andy finishes the house, will Jeanette actually like it?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on shows including SAS: Who Dares Wins, Dragons' Den, Three Families, Headspace: Guide to Sleep, Secret Crush, Lego Masters USA and This Morning. They also proffer their thoights on a BBC News report about taking holidays abroad as the pandemic rules on travel are relaxed

programme.title: SAS: Who Dares Wins
programme.desc: After a physically and psychologically intense introduction to their SAS selection, the obstacle for the 17 remaining recruits to overcome over the next 48 hours is fear, as they are sent deeper into the wild and unforgiving terrain of the Isle of Raasay in Scotland. They are put through their paces in a forward abseil, exposed to extreme cold in a water immersion exercise and required to operate as a team in a pitch-black disused tunnel

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson welcomes 24-year-old Sian from Norfolk, a petrol head who wants to find a man she can love more than her beloved car, and 33-year-old fashion entrepreneur Tansel from London. programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer

programme.title: Johnny Vegas: Carry on Glamping
programme.desc: The comedian's attempts to create a glamping site made up of repurposed old vehicles hit more problems when master craftsman Pauleo tells him his 1950s Maltese bus is not worth saving. Johnny buys another vintage camper van and decides this one is a surprise gift for Bev - now all he has to do is keep the secret from her. Meanwhile, the pair head to Yorkshire to check out a possible location for the glampsite, only for the rise of Covid-19 to threaten the entire enterprise

programme.title: Davina McCall: Sex, Myths and the Menopause
programme.desc: Davina McCall was 44 when she began to experience hot flushes, depression and confusion - symptoms she hid until she discovered her body was starting the transition to menopause. Here she describes her own experience to bust the myths surrounding the menopause, tackling the mid-life taboos that can destroy women's jobs and relationships, and exposing the lack of specialist education for GPs and the confusion that still exists around hormone replacement therapy

programme.title: Beat the Chef
programme.desc: Amateur cook Abbi Simms dishes up her sweet potato gnocchi in a challenge against chef Hrishikesh Desai, before the two dish up the chef's posh pie. Hosted by Andi Oliver

programme.desc: Jacob is the 19-year-old student son of millionaires, whose Chelsea flat was a gift from his parents. He goes to live with a cash-strapped couple in Manchester and their three children, and discovers that their weekly food budget matches one of his restaurant meals

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Another glimpse into the lives of expats who have left the UK for a new life in the Spanish sun. Cabaret star Mark - who appears on stage as Ruby Rox - films a video to play as part of his show, and sisters Vina and Nilam launch a business selling spice kits so expats can make their own curries. Tattoo artists Blane and Mark are in need of more customers, while retiree and metal detector enthusiast Stan spends his evenings searching the beaches for small change

programme.title: Paddington Station 24/7
programme.desc: The heat causes a fault in vital signalling equipment at Didcot, resulting in a points failure - and incident controller Greg has to react fast to prevent any snarl-ups. Cornwall engineers deal with a historic, but urgent, issue with increasingly unstable lines built in the 1860s in need of repair. The £1.5m project includes laying three concrete beams, the largest of which is 51 tonnes, to support the tracks, but, with just five nights to complete the works, it is a race against the clock to get job done

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: Cleaning duo Richard and Brennan take on some of Britain's filthiest and most cluttered homes. They begin with Lincolnshire single mum, Sam, who has dedicated her life to caring for her disabled daughter, leaving no time for cleaning

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: North Yorkshire's road crime team chases a gang stealing 4x4s from industrial estates every night, while West Yorkshire traffic cops pursue stolen vehicles across Bradford. In Leeds, a 19-year-old risks running across a busy road to meet his girlfriend and the resulting incident requires the full range of emergency services - including an air ambulance - to be deployed to try to save his life

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: North Yorkshire is considered a soft touch by criminals, who using the county's rural back roads to operate under cover of darkness. In this edition, the team's elite officers hunt down and arrest burglars, drug dealers and thieves. Jamie Theakston narrates

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers step in to deal with a rowdy reveller when things get out of hand on New Year's Eve in Lincoln city centre, while Suzie Ranyard catches out a motorist who claims he has been driving with a dodgy number plate for 18 years. Jon Moon arrests a shoplifter more than happy to admit to his crime and a young lad discovers how hard it is to pull the wool over the eyes of Jon Peacock

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Brian Conville gives a man a dressing-down for dangerous driving on the school run, and Barry Peach's detective skills come to the fore when he apprehends a fugitive who has been using a false identity. Meanwhile, during an initiative to clamp down on rural crime, officers from Lincolnshire's Roads Policing Unit find themselves pursuing some stolen tractors

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jon Moon and his dog Kanto are hot on the heels of some burglary suspects, while spot-checks by the unit on the A1 turn up a carrier bag full of cash. Jason Baxter rushes to the scene of an accident where the driver was thrown clear of the car, only for another crash to take place - this time involving a line of vehicles

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Doctors in the accident and emergency department treat a patient in cardiac arrest. Junior doctor George Kay leads the resus team, while Hannah Ryan steps in to head up the nurses. Meanwhile, cyclist Garry is rushed into hospital after a head-on collision with a metal gate, and elderly patient Larry comes to hospital with his wife Molly after he had a fall at home

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: The two Sheilas bond over family, and Hendrix signs up to help Mackenzie move Toadie's office, but makes a move that puts their friendship at risk

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Mac's incident turns out to be benign, but she needs mental health aid. Lewis gets into a fateful fight with Christian after losing his temper

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: The monumental task of cleaning the home of Andrew in Norfolk, who has filled his cluttered home with toys, models and computers. There are cat faeces throughout, and an infestation of bugs in the kitchen

programme.title: Rich Holiday, Poor Holiday
programme.desc: A group of wealthy friends from Guildford, who usually fly the globe together in search of sophisticated cultural experiences, take on a fast-paced budget Benidorm break. Meanwhile, party lovers from London who save every year to go to Benidorm together find themselves in the lap of luxury on a trip that sees them in the slowpaced confines of a five-star Caribbean hotel on the island of St Lucia. Each holiday is exactly what the respective groups normally avoid

programme.title: Cocktail (1988)

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Katie-Louise, who runs a buy-and-sell website for ex-pats, is on a mission to persuade businesses to donate for a charity auction to play out live on the radio. Meanwhile, Geoff, who has become the very first Mr Whippy of Benidorm, having bought a van and fitted it out with a soft ice cream machine, faces a potentially terminal problem when his generator blows up

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Another glimpse into the lives of expats who have left the UK for a new life in the Spanish sun. Cabaret star Mark - who appears on stage as Ruby Rox - films a video to play as part of his show, and sisters Vina and Nilam launch a business selling spice kits so expats can make their own curries. Tattoo artists Blane and Mark are in need of more customers, while retiree and metal detector enthusiast Stan spends his evenings searching the beaches for small change

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Another glimpse into the lives of expats who have left the UK for a new life in the Spanish sun. Former Glaswegian taxi drivers Pete and Kath came to Benidorm to retire, only to end up taking over a bar, while fitness instructor and entrepreneur Anthony has organised a campsite coach trip to the Algar waterfalls. Female impersonator James and his partner David have rented a working farm, and it proves a steep learning curve for the pair who have sunk every penny into the project

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: A 74-year-old with breathing difficulties is rushed into Barnsley Hospital, and registrar Dr Nick Mani considers placing the patient in a medically-induced coma to stabilise him. Junior doctor Ashleigh Trimble assesses 70-year-old Dave, whose daughter Sarah fears her dad's confusion and loss of appetite are masking a more serious condition

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Russell, a 72-year-old man is rushed into hospital by paramedics suffering from severe chest pains, while Dr Dyfrig Hughes treats Michael, who was brought in by ambulance after falling ten feet from a ladder while cutting hedges. Elsewhere, junior doctor George Kay has some exciting news to share with the team

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Doctors treat an elderly patient rushed into hospital suffering from a racing heartbeat, and a 28-year-old man receives treatment after a motorbike accident. Nurse Richard Jackson meets Graham, a patient with a complex medical history, who has been brought in with the symptoms of a stroke

programme.title: The Missing Files
programme.desc: This true crime documentary about the case of Milly Dowler looks in new detail at the role of the media, the police investigation into her disappearance and Levi Bellfield, the man who was eventually convicted of her murder. Why did it take police so long to look at Bellfield, who lived just yards from where Milly was last seen? And what was behind the tabloids' hacking of Milly's phone?

programme.title: Born to Kill?
programme.desc: A psychological profile of the former nurse, who is currently serving 13 life sentences for murdering four children and injuring nine others at Grantham Hospital in Lincolnshire in 1991. Psychologists and criminology experts explore the possible motives for her crimes, examining her behaviour during childhood, when she was known to be an attention seeker

programme.title: Murder in a Small Town
programme.desc: The re-examination of the case against Luke Mitchell unearths shocking revelations about two males who were identified by eyewitnesses at the scene where Jodi was murdered. The conduct of the police while interviewing Mitchell as a 14-year-old is called into question, and Luke and his mother Corinne take a lie-detector test

programme.title: When Kids Kill
programme.desc: Aaron Roach Bridgeman explores court restrictions and anonymity orders placed on child murderers, meaning the media cannot name perpetrators until the age of 18

programme.title: Murdered by My Daughter
programme.desc: Documentary examining the events surrounding the murders in April 2016 of Elizabeth and Katie Edwards by 14-year-olds Kim Edwards and Lucas Markham, Britain's youngest double killers. The officer in charge of the case gives an insight into the investigation, including the recording of the interview in which Kim explaining why she killed her mother and sister, and one in which her boyfriend detailed his involvement in the crime

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: The governing body of an exclusive men's club asks sleuthing scribe Jessica Fletcher to unmask the killer of a rogue member whom they suspect is one of the committee. Whodunnit, starring Angela Lansbury

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs faces an old adversary who has re-emerged with a plan to assassinate the US president and taken Kate hostage in a bid to ensure success. The FBI manages to foil the attack on the presidential helicopter, Marine One, but the case seems set to end on a downbeat note. Rudolf Martin guest stars, with Mark Harmon and Sasha Alexander

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy captain is held hostage in his own office while his wife and blind daughter are abducted, and the kidnappers try to extort a large sum of money from him for his family's release. The NCIS team is soon on the case, but initially struggles to identify the chief assailant

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the murder of a publishing executive last seen engaged in a heated argument over a taxi. They learn that the killing may have been a race hate crime, with their main suspect having a long history of prejudice. However, a technicality arises in the case, threatening to deny them the conviction. Starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate when a priest is found murdered in the confessional booth. The arrest of a teenager with a grudge seems to have them on the right track, but after discovering that the clergyman wasn't the killer's intended target they begin to focus their search on a petrol station cashier. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Conclusion of a three-part story. The investigation into a movie studio boss's murder becomes further complicated when fresh evidence points to yet another suspect. Meanwhile, the ex-husband of the deceased goes to trial to face accusations of having a hand in the crime - is an innocent man about to be convicted? Sam Waterston stars

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the case of a woman killed in a fall from her art studio rooftop. When the post-mortem reveals the presence of a drug in her system, detectives suspect she was doped - but there is a long list of suspects. Police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A private investigator's murder draws Briscoe and Green toward a case of corruption in a baseball league - where the manager will do anything to ensure his team wins. They learn the victim had unearthed a scandal in which an ineligible player was being fielded in every game - and his discovery may have led to his death. Detective drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Curtis investigate the murder of a model and discover splashes of blood that obviously did not come from the victim, but suggest the culprit is a relative. With Sam Waterston and Jerry Orbach

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs investigates the discovery of a woman's mummified remains, following a trail of clues that takes him and the team to Florida, where they search for another victim who mysteriously disappeared. One of their few leads is a manual on the secret of being a good wife - which eventually points them in the direction of a serial killer

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine helicopter is discovered abandoned in a crop circle with its pilots missing, and witnesses report seeing strange lights in the night sky, adding to the intrigue surrounding the case. Gibbs orders the sightings to be kept under wraps for fear of attracting the attention of UFO enthusiasts, before uncovering a dark secret involving local residents. Guest starring Timothy Bottoms (The Last Picture Show)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An investigation into the rape and murder of a woman revives harrowing memories for Ducky, who once worked on a similar case in which the killer was never captured and vowed to return. Bafflingly, both victims were civilians dressed in the uniform of a lieutenant, and each had a trident symbol carved into their neck. Starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Jimmy is torn between family and work after father-in-law Ed asks him to tamper with evidence from NCIS' current murder investigation. Ed Slater's poker friend, Anthony DiNozzo Sr, offers to help with the case. Brian Dietzen, Mark Harmon, David McCallum and Robert Wagner star

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Part two of two. The investigation into the disappearance and murder of a 16-year-old girl in New York City continues and the detectives discover more about the sordid family life of the judge and his son. However, as Goren and Logan come under scrutiny over their actions they realise their suspect is doing all he can to impede their inquiries into the teenager's death. Guest starring Colm Meaney

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: Logan and Barek investigate after a woman's mutilated body is found and learn she was a suspected drug smuggler who recently returned from a trip to Guatemala. Their inquiries take them to renowned plastic surgeon Dr Christine Hensel, who performed procedures on that region's poor inhabitants, but they realise she seems to be harbouring a secret. Guest starring Samantha Mathis

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Hawkes is persuaded by his girlfriend Camille to call in sick so he can join her at a food festival, but a stall explodes during the event - exposing his deception to his colleagues. The team's investigation reveals that something other than food was potentially being sold by the vendors, and a lead emerges with a link to another suspect

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: The detectives suspect a woman's murder could be the work of a serial killer, while Mac reconnects with his first partner, who is being stalked by an ex-con the pair put behind bars. Crime drama, guest starring Peter Fonda, with Gary Sinise

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: A ballerina tempts fate by appearing in a production thought to be cursed - and when she is discovered dead shortly after a performance, the superstitious rumours are seemingly proved valid. Whodunit, starring Angela Lansbury and Marisa Berenson

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: The Cabot Cove residents put on a play about a witch who cursed the town before being executed - which leads to an intriguing case for Jessica. Robert Vaughn guest stars alongside Angela Lansbury

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Exhausted by a European book tour, Jessica stops off to see an old friend who owns a luxurious Monte Carlo hotel, but it is not long before a murderer checks in. Ian Ogilvy guest stars, with Angela Lansbury

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica visits London, where she is introduced to an MP, a meeting that proves to be the kiss of death for the unfortunate fellow. However, the mystery surrounding his murder deepens as the corpse goes missing only to reappear with a new identity. Whodunit, guest starring Trevor Eve and Sharon Maughan

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: A daytime soap queen's husband is shot dead - but, luckily, one of her neighbours just happens to be amateur sleuth Jessica Fletcher. With Angela Lansbury. Guest starring Kate Mulgrew (Star Trek: Voyager)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica's sleuthing skills come into play as she sets out to clear a university colleague of a murder charge, after a man who apparently migrated to New York on a revenge mission is killed. Angela Lansbury and George Hearn star

programme.desc: Jessica tries to clear medic Seth Hazlitt after he is threatened with a possible malpractice suit when the owner of Cabot Cove's computer company dies in mysterious circumstances. Drama, starring Angela Lansbury

programme.desc: Benson helps two teenage sisters whose father has been murdered, and Rollins goes into labour. Drama chronicling the life and crimes of the New York Police Department, starring Mariska Hargitay and Kelli Giddish

programme.desc: As Stone takes on the case of a woman who killed her abusive husband, Benson and the team struggle with the idea of testifying against her

programme.desc: The 10th anniversary of the September 11, 2001, terrorist attacks arrives, prompting the forensic investigators to recall what they were each doing on the day. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise and Sela Ward

programme.title: Columbo: Uneasy Lies the Crown (1990)
programme.desc: A dentist with gambling debts discovers his wife is having an affair with one of his patients and wants a divorce. He plans to take revenge by poisoning the lover with his wife's heart medication, so she will be suspected - but he reckons without the attentions of the crumpled cop. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, James Read, Paul Burke, Jo Anderson and Nancy Walker

programme.title: Columbo Cries Wolf (1990)
programme.desc: The slovenly sleuth suspects the owner of a men's magazine is behind the strange disappearance of his business partner and former lover. The suspect insists the woman is still alive, while the detective struggles to prove his guilt owing to the absence of a body. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Ian Buchanan, Rebecca Staab, Deidre Hall and Alan Scarfe

programme.title: Columbo: Lady in Waiting (1971)
programme.desc: A desperate woman is determined to rid herself of her domineering brother and gain control of the family business, so hatches a plan to shoot him and claim she thought he was a burglar. Although her plan seems to have worked, her new-found power goes to her head - and she also has to contend with Columbo, who is convinced of her guilt. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Leslie Nielsen and Susan Clark

programme.title: Columbo: Murder with Too Many Notes (2000)
programme.desc: Oscar-winning composer Findlay Crawford relies on his assistant Gabriel to do all the hard work, but the younger musician demands recognition. To protect his reputation, Findlay kills Gabriel with a time-delayed drug to give himself the perfect alibi - but the slovenly sleuth sees through his ruse. Detective drama, with Peter Falk and Billy Connolly

programme.title: Columbo: Lovely but Lethal (1973)
programme.desc: A cosmetics company is rocked by the revelation that a pirated version of their latest product is on the market, and then, shortly afterwards, one of their chemists is found murdered. But when the shabby sleuth scrutinises the case, he suspects the events are linked to a rival company. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Vincent Price, Martin Sheen and Vera Miles

programme.title: Columbo: Mind Over Mayhem (1974)
programme.desc: A scientist is overjoyed when his son follows in his footsteps, receiving widespread acclaim for a revolutionary new theory. When a fellow boffin produces evidence of plagiarism, the ruthless father resorts to murder to save his son from a scandal. But he reckons without the dishevelled detective, who sees through his efforts to make the killing look like the work of burglars. Crime drama, with Peter Falk, Jose Ferrer and Lew Ayres

programme.title: Columbo: Blueprint for Murder (1972)
programme.desc: An architect kills his business partner, and comes up with an elaborate scheme to make Columbo first suspect him, then reject him as the murderer. The villain begins by teasing the detective with hints on where he has hidden the body - hoping that when Columbo fails to find a corpse, he will look elsewhere for a killer. Crime drama, directed by and starring Peter Falk. With Patrick O'Neal and Janis Paige

programme.title: Columbo: Ransom for a Dead Man (1971)
programme.desc: A devious lawyer hatches the perfect murder plot - she kills her husband, tells the police he has been kidnapped and leaves the body at the ransom drop, making it look like the criminals who abducted him finished him off. However, the crumpled cop sees through her ruse and begins picking apart her story in his inimitable manner. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Lee Grant and Patricia Mattick

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and the team investigate the death of a prostitute at a pop-up brothel. The detectives contemplate if there was foul play was at work or whether this could be a case of a junkie who relapsed, turned to tricking, got depressed and committed suicide. Drama, starring Mariska Hargitay, Kelli Giddish and Ice-T

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: Benson and the SVU team investigate the death of a man they believe to be a pedophile. When they dig deeper, they realise things are not what they seem, and as matters progress the case actually hits quite close to home. Drama, starring Mariska Hargitay and Kelli Giddish

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and the team investigate the murder of a college student, focusing their inquiry on his best friend, who has been printing counterfeit money - but evidence suggests another party may have been involved. programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Claire Rawle and Michael Baggott value a selection of the public's antiques in Longleat, Wiltshire, including a 1950s aquarium lighter, Chinese incense pots and collectable film posters. Meanwhile, presenter Paul Martin takes a dip in the River Frome with one of the oldest swimming clubs in the country

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team visit Layer Marney Tower in rural Essex, where antiques experts Elizabeth Talbot and Philip Serrell examine items brought in by members of the public, including a Clarice Cliff coffee set and a bronze model of an Alsatian dog. Paul goes in search of the Witchfinder General, one of the county's most notorious characters

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Paul Laidlaw scour Somerset for valuables and head for auction in Hampshire, with Paul's buys including items from America, Africa and Staffordshire

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw and Kate Bliss go head-to-head at an auction in Windsor, where Paul comes across a tank, and Kate hopes to make a profit with a mechanical penguin

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: After years spent dedicated to their demanding careers in nursing and social work, Gillian and Paul Clarke from Stockport have decided it's time to start taking it a little easier and put themselves first. They've got their hearts set on a holiday home on Spain's Costa Cálida where they can boogie their way to retirement. Jonnie Irwin shows the couple five properties for their budget of £45,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: After becoming foster parents to three young children, Jo Danson and her husband Bill decided a holiday home would be the perfect way for everyone to spend quality time together. Working within a budget of £65,000, Jasmine Harman shows Jo five suitable properties in the Orihuela Costa region of Spain

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Angus Ashworth and the team clear out a huge aviation collection in Co Durham, then help a retired policeman sell items from his four-bedroom home

programme.title: Helicopter ER
programme.desc: The team joins firefighters trying to rescue the victim of an 11,000-volt electric shock, while an elderly motorist needs help after a bizarre accident

programme.title: Helicopter ER
programme.desc: The Yorkshire Air Ambulance crew is called to rescue a man trapped under a tractor that has plunged off a bridge and is in danger of sinking. Paramedic Paul is concerned his patient may go into cardiac arrest - and there's a real chance he may drown if firefighters cannot free him. The chopper lands on a farm to save a farmer who's been attacked by his own herd of cattle. Plus, on a road over the North York Moors, a wild deer runs out in front of a biker and leaves him with serious injuries

programme.title: Helicopter ER
programme.desc: A desperate search is staged for a factory worker's missing arm after a horrific industrial accident. A man on a family outing plunges down a rockface on front of his terrified wife and daughter at one of Yorkshire's best known landmarks. Elsewhere, the team is called to rescue an elderly passenger trapped after a car skids on an icy hill and overturns

programme.title: Helicopter ER
programme.desc: A herd of cows goes rogue and turns on its stockman, leaving him with life threatening injuries. The paramedics of the Yorkshire Air Ambulance also fall victim to an elaborate hoax after a climber who claims to have fallen off a cliff face turns out to be unhurt

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: In Ireland, the Castletownbere crew race to rescue a lone yachtsman, who has lost his mast and is drifting 45 miles out to sea. Meanwhile, a team search for a woman in Minehead who has gone missing on a cliff top walk, and for an injured horse rider on a remote beach, the Porthcawl crew are the only way to emergency medical help

programme.title: Cops UK: Bodycam Squad
programme.desc: Documentary series following the work of the Nottinghamshire Police force through officers' body-worn cameras

programme.title: Help! My House Is Haunted
programme.desc: Sandy Lakdar, Barri Ghai and Chris Fleming explore a country estate in Devon with a dark history, from battles on the grounds to the spirit of a tormented child

programme.title: Ghost Adventures
programme.desc: Zak Bagans and the crew return to Virginia City to investigate an infamous saloon where employees claim to have been violently attacked by evil spirits

programme.title: Portals to Hell
programme.desc: Jack Osbourne and Katrina Weidman investigate reports of paranormal activity at the Fulton Theatre in Pennsylvania, which was built on the site of a horrific massacre

programme.title: Kindred Spirits
programme.desc: Paranormal investigators Amy Bruni and Adam Berry visit a Virginian plantation, where an evil overseer who died in the Civil War is thought to have returned from the grave

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Animal Cops Phoenix
programme.desc: Investigator Marisa McHugh and Field Operations Manager Stacie DaBolt confront the owner of six emaciated dogs suffering from mange

programme.title: Animal Cops Phoenix
programme.desc: Field operations manager DaBolt and investigator Barkley respond to reports of an illegal dog fight and discover three pitbulls urgently need their help

programme.title: Fantasy Homes by the Sea
programme.desc: Presenter Catherine Gee helps best friends Lorraine and Angela, from Kent, find a holiday home in Corfu on a budget of £180,000

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Teleshopping Feature. Your one-stop-shop for all things cruise; bringing you the hottest destinations, ground-breaking ships and the latest deals to ensure your next cruise is your greatest one

programme.title: Fantasy Homes by the Sea
programme.desc: Jenni Falconer helps a family make a fresh start as they search for a permanent home on Dorset's Jurassic coast

programme.title: Fantasy Homes by the Sea
programme.desc: Jenni Falconer helps a Cambridgeshire couple downsize to a home by the sea in East Sussex for £500,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Manjit and Cathy Jandu have recently retired from their medical practice and are ready for a new environment. Inspired by their son who lived in Madrid for a year, they've decided Spain is for them and are determined to buy a villa around Estepona on the Costa del Sol. With £300,000 to find the perfect property, Jonnie Irwin sets out to locate and show them five homes to choose between

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Recently divorced Patricia Malty from Devon wants to make a fresh start on the Costa Calida in Spain after selling her home and moving in with her best friend, with Laura Hamilton helping her find suitable properties for her £90,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Recently retired couple Nick and Jane Purser are looking forward to settling down, but cannot decide where that should be. Jane's mother lives in Liverpool, so she would prefer to stay close by with a move to Lancaster. Nick wants them to move to southern France, where he can ride his motorbike in the sun. Jasmine Harman shows the pair three properties in Lancaster for up to £180,000 and Jonnie Irwin tempts them with three properties for up to £145,000 in France

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: When her partner of 20 years died, Ginny Lunn decided to find a holiday home where she could create new memories. Torn between buying a place in Dorset within easy reach of her London home for a budget of £250,000, and the Costa Tropical in Spain, where she would have a budget of £150,000, she requires help. Jasmine Harman shows her three options in Dorset, and Laura Hamilton tries to impress her with three properties in Spain, but, with the help of her sister Sara, which one will Ginny choose?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Juliette and Jonathan Cottam are selling their London home to retire elsewhere, but are torn between two very different locations. Juliette dreams of a life in France, where she spent much of her childhood. With a budget of £250,000, she thinks the Dordogne region would be the perfect location. Jonathan wants to stay in the UK with a budget of up to £350,000 and move to Devon where he grew up. Jasmine Harman tempts Juliette with three properties in Devon, while Lara Hamilton shows them three fantastic French homes

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Paul Laidlaw both find unusual powder compacts, making for an exciting auction in Brighton. Paul also visits a mosque and Kate tries gardening, Victorian-style

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Paul Laidlaw's final trip in the 1960s Volvo sees them plot a course through Kent. Paul finds himself in Dover and hears about one plucky swimmer who believed bagpipes would help him across the English Channel, while Kate infiltrates a once top secret organisation for the afternoon to learn more about their history. The duo later head for an auction in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Natasha Raskin Sharp set off in their 1969 Morris Minor from the Firth of Clyde in search of bargains, making their way through the Scottish Borders to an auction in Carlisle

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Angus Ashworth and the team clear out a huge aviation collection in Co Durham, then help a retired policeman sell items from his four-bedroom home

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: The experts head for Derbyshire, where Charles Hanson is on his home turf, enlisting the aid of his two-year-old daughter and hitching a ride on a horse and cart before investing in a collection of medieval items dredged from a river bed. Meanwhile, Natasha Raskin Sharp visits the world's oldest lift shaft

programme.title: Cash in the Spare Room
programme.desc: A man from Fort William is looking for a new source of income after his wife sadly passed away. He enlists Max to help transform his plot into a money-spinning holiday let

programme.title: Dr Pimple Popper
programme.desc: Tim has a massive growth on his back that affects his ability to work and socialise. Meanwhile, a mother and daughter have bonded over their bodies

programme.title: Faking It: Tears of a Crime
programme.desc: Harold Shipman was a much-loved doctor. Millions watched the GP speaking on TV discussing the benefits of care, not knowing he'd already killed dozens of patients

programme.title: My Violent Child
programme.desc: Laverne Antrobus tries to help Emma and Adrian, who are struggling to cope with eight-year-old son Ioan, whose violent behaviour means getting him to bed can take up to six hours. Laverne is worried both by Ioan's violence and by Emma's use of restraints, so she institutes a new bedtime routine, and helps Adrian to strengthen his relationship with Ioan by getting out of the house and doing outdoor activities

programme.title: Emergency

programme.title: Motorway Patrol
programme.desc: Documentary following members of the New Zealand highway patrol as they witness some of the best and worst behaviour on the nation's motorways. The series is centred around the roads of Auckland, the country's largest urban area, and features motoring chaos including major traffic collisions, escaped animals and pedestrians on busy roads. There is also footage of drivers falling asleep at the wheel, overloaded vehicles and suspects caught with illegal substances - plus a host of excuses as the people pulled over try to persuade officers they have not done anything wrong

programme.title: Neighbourhood Blues
programme.desc: An officer's hunch helps catch a wanted man, while in Weston, the team responds to a report of mini motos causing a nuisance, however it is unclear who owns the bikes. Elsewhere, the police team up with Trading Standards in a bid to remove legal highs from the shops and streets of Somerset, and an officer chases a bus to catch a criminal who smashed up an arcade

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Return of the show in which Dominic Littlewood reveals the innovative methods the police and members of the public are using to catch criminals in the act. In the first edition, CCTV cameras are used in a bid to help identify someone who has been making hoax calls to the emergency services, and the presenter meets an angry daughter who has hatched a clever plan to catch the carer she believes has been stealing from her parents

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood recounts the tale of a burglar who jumped from an upstairs window after being confronted by a homeowner, but accidently left behind an item of clothing later used as evidence against him. Plus, how police employ the latest technology to catch speeding motorcyclists

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood investigates the innovative ways the police and members of the public are catching criminals in the act, featuring a police operation to track down thieves who were filmed on CCTV as they callously tricked shopkeepers and remarkable footage of fly-tippers who were caught hook, line and sinker

programme.title: Stop, Search, Seize
programme.desc: Documentary following the work of Ireland's border force officers as they fight to stop gangs of organised smugglers from bringing drugs, money, guns, cars and even exotic animals into the country

programme.title: The Force: North East
programme.desc: Bonfire night brings back-to-back emergency calls, as masked youths hurl fireworks at officers out on patrol and a search for a missing girl takes an unusual turn

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The crimes of Philip Austin, who murdered his wife, two children and two dogs on one single, terrifying day

programme.title: How I Caught the Killer
programme.desc: The documentary series reveals how a computer at Vogue magazine held the key to identifying Julie Clayton, an ex-cult member who had been assaulted and murdered upon leaving

programme.title: Britain's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The crimes of Colin Ireland, a cold and calculated serial killer who brutally snatched away the lives of five innocent men

programme.title: Killer in My Village
programme.desc: A look into the investigation of 16-year-old Leanne Tiernan's murder. In November 2000, she failed to return home from a shopping trip in Leeds

programme.title: Britcam: Emergency on Our Streets
programme.desc: Documentary providing an insight into the lives of British people through the use of mobile body cameras attached to public-facing professionals. Leicestershire Police's rapid response team try to defuse a difficult situation at a nightclub, while immediate care specialist Andy helps a girl who has driven her car into a tree

programme.title: Police Force: Australia
programme.desc: Documentary series following the work of police forces across Australia as they deal with situations from sieges and major drug busts, to murder investigations

programme.desc: Zara wants her pet caterpillar to perform in a special circus

programme.title: Zigby
programme.desc: The excitable zebra encourages everyone to share their belongings, but the scheme does not go as planned

programme.title: Pocket Dragon Adventures
programme.desc: A group of very small dragons live with a kindly old wizard, they're not big enough to fly but that doesn't stop them having many adventures

programme.title: UBOS
programme.desc: Cassy, Vern & Gus are gifted students who go to an enchanted school to learn about magic. After receiving a book named UBOS (Ultimate Book of Spells) they soon begin their journey of a lifetime

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: The king wants to demolish the village and build a palace on the site, leaving the vikings homeless

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Ylva competes against Baltak's wife in a baking contest - and they are both send their husbands on a quest to find a very rare ingredient

programme.title: Dork Hunters from Outer Space
programme.desc: A group of heroes try to stop aliens taking over the universe

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the resistance movement set up around the coast by Winston Churchill to harass Nazi invaders should the Germans reach British soil

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the resistance movement set up around the coast by Winston Churchill to harass Nazi invaders should the Germans reach British soil

programme.title: The Dales
programme.desc: Actor and comedian Adrian Edmondson returns to his birthplace of Yorkshire to document a summer in the life of the Dales, meeting the people who live there all year round. Sixteen-year-old Phillip Mellin talks about running his family's farm alongside his mother, following the death of his father last year, while vet Neil Roberts is called out to an emergency calving

programme.title: The Dales
programme.desc: Farmer Alex Wilkes takes her small herd of dairy cows to compete at the Great Yorkshire Show in Harrogate, and Adrian Edmondson meets Tom Orde-Powlett, who is the heir to the historic Bolton Castle in Wensleydale. Plus, 16-year-old Phillip Mellin tries to shear sheep for the first time

programme.title: Iron Men (2017)
programme.desc: Go behind the scenes with the fans of West Ham United as they move to a new home after 112 years at Upton Park. Featuring Slaven Bilic, Mark Noble Mabel Arnold and Ray Winstone

programme.title: This Is London
programme.desc: From the London Screen Archives, shot on 8mm film in 1980. A wonderful travelogue from Pauline Harrison that starts with a tourists view of London

programme.title: Maria Marten: Or, Murder in the Red Barn (1935)
programme.desc: An evil squire lures a woman into marriage, but when she becomes pregnant, he decides to murder her, leaving a young gypsy who is in love with her to take the blame. Period drama, starring Tod Slaughter and Sophie Stewart

programme.title: Spring and Port Wine (1970)
programme.desc: A tyrannically strict Lancashire father brings about the downfall of his family by forcing his daughter to eat a meal against her will. Drama, starring James Mason, Diana Coupland, Susan George and Rodney Bewes

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Series featuring inspirational walks between 2-8 miles from around Britain. Andrew White will guide you through the best walkways that Great Britain has to offer.

programme.title: St Paul's
programme.desc: Part two. The Dean and Chapter are forced to consider increasing admission cost to help maintain the building

programme.title: London Live News
programme.desc: News headlines, travel, weather and debate from across the capital

programme.title: Britain's Greatest Generation
programme.desc: The Second World War saw men from all classes unite to fight the Nazis, and this programme meets some of the last surviving pilots of the Battle of Britain. Women, who had been excluded from many jobs, worked in everything from anti-aircraft gun batteries to steel-making factories. The Blitz saw rescue workers risk all to save those trapped in bombed buildings. For many of the era's last survivors, the defiant spirit that brought the nation together remains one of their proudest moments, as this documentary reveals

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Peter Maneas embarks on a journey through some of the most spectacular and unique Greek islands, exploring the rich culture, history and food of Greece while meeting the locals and those who live there

programme.title: The Old Guys
programme.desc: Comedy series, starring Clive Swift and Roger Lloyd Pack, about two lively pensioners who see their age as no barrier to having fun. Tom and Roy try to get themselves invited to a party hosted by their sexy neighbour Sally, played by Jane Asher, by promising to help with the preparations. But things do not go according to plan when Tom has a fall and Roy is needed to nurse him - compromising his role as party organiser

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: The lonely residents of the street find company with each other as Christmas arrives. Comedy, written and performed by John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: Hot Property (2016)
programme.desc: A corporate spy loses her job after getting caught embezzling, and is no longer able to fund her lavish lifestyle. Faced with being evicted from her rented home, she puts all her skills of deception into fending off the landlord, replacement tenants and a psychotic estate agent. Comedy, starring MyAnna Buring and Tom Rhys Harries

programme.title: Eugenie De Sade (1973)
programme.desc: A teenage girl lives alone with her stepfather after the death of her mother, and becomes fascinated with the dark erotic stories he writes. As she grows more and more sexually obsessed with him and his tales, she fails to realise the dangerous secrets that have inspired his creations. Erotic thriller, starring Soledad Miranda and Paul Muller

programme.title: The Crouches
programme.desc: Natalie still wants a little more out of life, so when the handsome Rev Garstang invites her along to a classical music concert, she jumps at the chance. Naturally, Roly's not too pleased about this turn of events, so he decides to confront the man threatening his marriage - only to end up admitting that if he were Natalie, he'd want to sleep with the newcomer too

programme.title: St Paul's
programme.desc: Part two. The Dean and Chapter are forced to consider increasing admission cost to help maintain the building

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tour exploring and revealing Shadwell and Grove Park for locals and tourists. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.desc: A digest of news and events from across London from the past few days

programme.title: Talking Points

programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Manganinnie (1980)
programme.desc: An Australian Aboriginal woman goes in search of her lost tribe, accompanied on her journey by a lost white girl. with whom she forms a close bond despite having no common language. As their journey towards the coast continues, the woman begins to suspect her people and culture may have been all but wiped out by British colonists. Historical drama, starring Mawuyul Yanthalawuy and Anna Ralph

programme.title: The Belstone Fox (1973)
programme.desc: A huntsman adopts a fox cub into his pack of hounds, and the animal becomes a local celebrity, until a disastrous hunt turns his human friends against him. Wildlife adventure based on David Rook's novel, starring Eric Porter, Bill Travers and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: One Good Turn (1954)
programme.desc: A boy living in an orphanage grows up to be the institution's lovable but clumsy caretaker. He must save the children's home from a wicked property developer who wants to build a factory on the site, and tricks him into taking the kids to the seaside so he can knock the building down. Comedy, starring Norman Wisdom and Joan Rice

programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the resistance movement set up around the coast by Winston Churchill to harass Nazi invaders should the Germans reach British soil

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the resistance movement set up around the coast by Winston Churchill to harass Nazi invaders should the Germans reach British soil

programme.title: This Could Go Anywhere
programme.desc: Phil Tufnell and Brendon McCullum travel all over New Zealand - playing golf and discovering the country's most famous and picturesque courses

programme.title: The Crouches
programme.desc: Natalie still wants a little more out of life, so when the handsome Rev Garstang invites her along to a classical music concert, she jumps at the chance. Naturally, Roly's not too pleased about this turn of events, so he decides to confront the man threatening his marriage - only to end up admitting that if he were Natalie, he'd want to sleep with the newcomer too

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Michael Caine buys a birthday present for Mrs Huggett. The lives, loves and laundry of the famous stars living in leafy Surbiton, as performed by impressionists John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: Kidnapping Freddy Heineken (2015)
programme.desc: Five men struggling to make ends meet decide to kidnap a beer tycoon who is one of Europe's richest men. The plan is a success, and they demand the biggest ransom in history - but tensions within the group threaten their chances of escaping the law. Fact-based crime drama, starring Anthony Hopkins, Jim Sturgess and Sam Worthington

programme.title: Mad Dog Morgan (1976)
programme.desc: An Irishman seeking his fortune in the Australian Gold Rush ends up in prison when driven to robbery out of desperation. After 12 years of abuse, he is released and becomes an outlaw, determined to make society pay for his suffering - and becoming a hero to the downtrodden in the process. Fact-based Western, starring Dennis Hopper and Jack Thompson

programme.title: Obsessed with My Body
programme.desc: With British boys now apparently spending 30 per cent more time in the bathroom than teenage girls and taking twice as many selfies, this documentary follows some of those in search of the perfect body. From bodybuilding and eyebrow waxing to make-up, the film also examines how far they will they go to achieve perfection. And what happens when things are taken to extremes?

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Series featuring inspirational walks between from around Britain. Andrew White walks on a disused railway in the Peak District and alongside the Caledonian Canal

programme.title: Secret Army
programme.desc: Peter Williams tells the story of the resistance movement set up around the coast by Winston Churchill to harass Nazi invaders should the Germans reach British soil

programme.title: Spring and Port Wine (1970)
programme.desc: A tyrannically strict Lancashire father brings about the downfall of his family by forcing his daughter to eat a meal against her will. Drama, starring James Mason, Diana Coupland, Susan George and Rodney Bewes

programme.title: The Best of Men
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about the birth of the Paralympics in 1948. Pioneering neurologist Dr Ludwig Guttmann arrives at Stoke Mandeville Hospital in Buckinghamshire and begins to transform the lives of his patients - Second World War servicemen with spinal cord injuries. He suggests using sport as a key part of their rehabilitation, and gets them involved in activities including wheelchair polo and basketball. Starring Eddie Marsan, Rob Brydon, George Mackay and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: The Face at the Window (1939)
programme.desc: In 1880, Paris is terrorised by a hideous murderer. An innocent man is accused of being the killer by a respected aristocrat who longs to get him out of the way so he can seduce his fiancee - but may also have other reasons for wanting a scapegoat. Horror, starring Tod Slaughter and John Warwick

programme.title: The Getting of Wisdom (1977)
programme.desc: A snobbish Victorian college for young ladies is thrown into disarray by the arrival of a strong-willed girl from the Outback. Australian drama, starring Barry Humphries, Susannah Fowle, John Waters and Terence Donovan

programme.title: The Slipper and the Rose (1976)
programme.desc: A girl is reduced to being the downtrodden servant of her cruel stepmother after her father's death. With a little help from her fairy godmother, she wins the heart of a handsome prince, who is desperately trying to avoid being forced into a political marriage by his parents. Musical version of Cinderella, starring Gemma Craven and Richard Chamberlain

programme.title: Obsessed with My Body
programme.desc: With British boys now apparently spending 30 per cent more time in the bathroom than teenage girls and taking twice as many selfies, this documentary follows some of those in search of the perfect body. From bodybuilding and eyebrow waxing to make-up, the film also examines how far they will they go to achieve perfection. And what happens when things are taken to extremes?

programme.desc: Peter Maneas embarks on a journey through some of the most spectacular and unique Greek islands, exploring the rich culture, history and food of Greece while meeting the locals and those who live there

programme.title: The Dales
programme.desc: Actor and comedian Adrian Edmondson returns to his birthplace of Yorkshire to document a summer in the life of the Dales, meeting the people who live there all year round

programme.title: National Treasure
programme.desc: Paul and his family have to deal with the pressure of mounting allegations against him, and Dee confronts her former babysitter, Christina, now one of the star's accusers. Marie is rattled to discover she was away on the nights Paul is accused of assaulting her, and the accused gives a radio interview, determined to put his side of the story. Drama, starring Robbie Coltrane and Julie Walters

programme.title: Man About the House (1974)
programme.desc: Student chef Robin and his two female flatmates discover the council is planning to demolish the terrace where they live. Their landlord is happy to take the money being offered for his property, but his wife sides with the tenants in fighting the plan. Movie version of the 1970s sitcom, starring Richard O'Sullivan, Paula Wilcox, Sally Thomsett, Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy

programme.title: Helga, She Wolf of Stilberg (1978)
programme.desc: A sadistic female Nazi officer runs a women's prison during the Second World War, and forces the inmates to be her sexual playthings. However, the latest arrival, a determined resistance fighter, proves to be far more difficult to dominate. Prison drama, starring Malisa Longo and Patrizia Gori. In French

programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner
